After the Empress died together with her older brother Sahohiko in the rebellion provoked by himself, the Emperor took Hibasuhime as the second Empress, and the first son Inishikiiribiko no mikoto, and the second son Otarashihiko no mikoto (Emperor Keiko) were born.
In the 30th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, when the Emperor asked each one of these two brothers what they wanted, the older brother Inishikiiribiko no mikoto wanted 'bows and arrows' and the smaller brother Otarashihiko no mikoto wanted the 'Imperial Throne,' and in this way, it was decided that Otarashihiko no mikoto would be the successor to Emperor Suinin.
In the ninth month of the 35th year of the same reign, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was dispatched to Kawachi Province and he developed many irrigation ponds and ditches, including Takashi no ike Pond and Chinu no ike Pond. It is said that he also promoted agricultural development, traveling extensively from province to province, in order to stabilize people's livelihood, and therefore, he was highly respected by the people.
In the tenth month of the 39th year of the same reign, he made one thousand swords at Utonokawakami no miya Shrine, which was possibly in the vicinity of present Jinenda, Hannan City, Osaka Prefecture). And he dedicated them to Ishigami-jinja Shrine. Emperor Suinin ordered Inishikiiribiko no mikoto to take control over the sacred treasures of the Shrine.
In the second month of the 87th year of the reign, he appointed Onakatsuhime to manage the sacred treasures for his old age. Onakatsuhime granted the sacred treasures to MONONOBE no Tochine and made him take care of them. This is said to have been the reason why the sacred treasures of Ishigami-jinja Shrine were under the custody of the Mononobe clan.
Legend about his end According to the legend handed down at Inaba-jinja Shrine in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto, once dominated Oshu Province on the Imperial edict, however, as Governor of Mutsu Province, Toyomasu, who accompanied him to Oshu, became jealous of Inishikiiribiko no mikoto's success and informed the Imperial court of a false charge that Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was plotting a treason, and thus, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was accused as an Imperial enemy and attacked to death there. When his wife, Nunoshihime no mikoto knew her husband's death, she went down to Oshu, leaving the capital, and single-mindedly longed for her late husband, devoting herself in prayer to console the spirit of the mikoto until she died. Inishikiiribiko no mikoto is highly respected even today as the shusaijin (main enshrined deities) of Inama-jinja Shrine.
For your reference, it should be noted that according to "Tensho" (a chronological history of Japan), which is said to have been a falsified record in the Edo Period, his date of birth was the 24th day of the 2nd month of the 93rd year of Emperor Suinin's reign (old lunar calendar). However in this book there is no description related to the engi (writing about the history) mentioned above.
According to the legend transmitted at Ishigami-jinja Shrine, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was living next to Ishigami-jinja Shrine, in the premises situated to the southeast of the haiden (hall of worship), and when he got senile, he wanted his younger sister Onakatsuhime to take the charge of the religious service. However, she refused his offer saying that she would not like to climb up to a higher floor (elevated bridge) because she was just a woman; then he had no idea other than appointing MONONOBE no Tochine, who was in the position of Omuraji (one of the important official ranks of Yamato Imperial Court, representing the local ruling lords called Muraji) (the religious service referred to here might have included the task of getting in and out the sacred treasures). About the chronicle of achievements mentioned above, the same episode about him is found in the article of the second month of the 87th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. It is said that he died of old age.
== Mausoleum == Udohaka Tomb is said to be his mausoleum. (it is also called Tannowa nisanzai Tomb).
It is located in Tannowa, Misaki-cho, Sennan-gun, Osaka Prefecture. It is a big-scale keyhole-shaped mound with the total length of 200m, which was constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. Although it is said to be Inishiki no mikoto's tomb, there is no evidence and it is currently under the control of the Imperial Household Agency. The nearest station is Tannowa Station on the Nankai Main Line.

revision.text:
Inishikiiribiko no mikoto (五十瓊敷入彦命) was a member of the Imperial family (Royal family), who is said to have been lived in the Kofun period (tumulus period), according to "Kojiki " (The Record of Ancient Matters) and "Nihonshoki" (Chronicles of Japan). He was the second Imperial Prince of Emperor Suinin (the first Imperial Prince was Homutsuwake no mikoto), the son born between Emperor Suinin and Sahohime). He was also mentioned as Iinishiki no mikoto (五十瓊敷命) and Inishikinoirohiko no mikoto (印色入日子命) ("Kojiki").
== Achievements Based on the chronology employed in the Nihonshoki. == Emperor Suinin had the Empress called Sahohime. After the Empress died together with her older brother Sahohiko in the rebellion provoked by himself, the Emperor took Hibasuhime as the second Empress, and the first son Inishikiiribiko no mikoto, and the second son Otarashihiko no mikoto (Emperor Keiko) were born.
In the 30th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, when the Emperor asked each one of these two brothers what they wanted, the older brother Inishikiiribiko no mikoto wanted 'bows and arrows' and the smaller brother Otarashihiko no mikoto wanted the 'Imperial Throne,' and in this way, it was decided that Otarashihiko no mikoto would be the successor to Emperor Suinin.
In the ninth month of the 35th year of the same reign, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was dispatched to Kawachi Province and he developed many irrigation ponds and ditches, including Takashi no ike Pond and Chinu no ike Pond. It is said that he also promoted agricultural development, traveling extensively from province to province, in order to stabilize people's livelihood, and therefore, he was highly respected by the people.
In the tenth month of the 39th year of the same reign, he made one thousand swords at Utonokawakami no miya Shrine, which was possibly in the vicinity of present Jinenda, Hannan City, Osaka Prefecture). And he dedicated them to Ishigami-jinja Shrine. Emperor Suinin ordered Inishikiiribiko no mikoto to take control over the sacred treasures of the Shrine.
In the second month of the 87th year of the reign, he appointed Onakatsuhime to manage the sacred treasures for his old age. Onakatsuhime granted the sacred treasures to MONONOBE no Tochine and made him take care of them. This is said to have been the reason why the sacred treasures of Ishigami-jinja Shrine were under the custody of the Mononobe clan.
Legend about his end According to the legend handed down at Inaba-jinja Shrine in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto, once dominated Oshu Province on the Imperial edict, however, as Governor of Mutsu Province, Toyomasu, who accompanied him to Oshu, became jealous of Inishikiiribiko no mikoto's success and informed the Imperial court of a false charge that Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was plotting a treason, and thus, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was accused as an Imperial enemy and attacked to death there. When his wife, Nunoshihime no mikoto knew her husband's death, she went down to Oshu, leaving the capital, and single-mindedly longed for her late husband, devoting herself in prayer to console the spirit of the mikoto until she died. Inishikiiribiko no mikoto is highly respected even today as the shusaijin (main enshrined deities) of Inama-jinja Shrine.
For your reference, it should be noted that according to "Tensho" (a chronological history of Japan), which is said to have been a falsified record in the Edo Period, his date of birth was the 24th day of the 2nd month of the 93rd year of Emperor Suinin's reign (old lunar calendar). However in this book there is no description related to the engi (writing about the history) mentioned above.
According to the legend transmitted at Ishigami-jinja Shrine, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was living next to Ishigami-jinja Shrine, in the premises situated to the southeast of the haiden (hall of worship), and when he got senile, he wanted his younger sister Onakatsuhime to take the charge of the religious service. However, she refused his offer saying that she would not like to climb up to a higher floor (elevated bridge) because she was just a woman; then he had no idea other than appointing MONONOBE no Tochine, who was in the position of Omuraji (one of the important official ranks of Yamato Imperial Court, representing the local ruling lords called Muraji) (the religious service referred to here might have included the task of getting in and out the sacred treasures). About the chronicle of achievements mentioned above, the same episode about him is found in the article of the second month of the 87th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. It is said that he died of old age.
== Mausoleum == Udohaka Tomb is said to be his mausoleum. (it is also called Tannowa nisanzai Tomb).
It is located in Tannowa, Misaki-cho, Sennan-gun, Osaka Prefecture. It is a big-scale keyhole-shaped mound with the total length of 200m, which was constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. Although it is said to be Inishiki no mikoto's tomb, there is no evidence and it is currently under the control of the Imperial Household Agency. The nearest station is Tannowa Station on the Nankai Main Line.

About the chronicle of achievements mentioned above, the same episode about him is found in the article of the second month of the 87th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. It is said that he died of old age.
== Mausoleum == Udohaka Tomb is said to be his mausoleum. (it is also called Tannowa nisanzai Tomb).
It is located in Tannowa, Misaki-cho, Sennan-gun, Osaka Prefecture. It is a big-scale keyhole-shaped mound with the total length of 200m, which was constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. Although it is said to be Inishiki no mikoto's tomb, there is no evidence and it is currently under the control of the Imperial Household Agency. The nearest station is Tannowa Station on the Nankai Main Line.

{{draft categories| [[Category:Prince Inishiki]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Itsuninomikoto]] [[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Itsuninomikoto}}

Inishikiiribiko no mikoto (五十瓊敷入彦命) was a member of the Imperial family (Royal family), who is said to have been lived in the Kofun period (tumulus period), according to "Kojiki " (The Record of Ancient Matters) and "Nihonshoki" (Chronicles of Japan). He was the second Imperial Prince of Emperor Suinin (the first Imperial Prince was Homutsuwake no mikoto), the son born between Emperor Suinin and Sahohime). He was also mentioned as Iinishiki no mikoto (五十瓊敷命) and Inishikinoirohiko no mikoto (印色入日子命) ("Kojiki").
== Achievements Based on the chronology employed in the Nihonshoki. == Emperor Suinin had the Empress called Sahohime. After the Empress died together with her older brother Sahohiko in the rebellion provoked by himself, the Emperor took Hibasuhime as the second Empress, and the first son Inishikiiribiko no mikoto, and the second son Otarashihiko no mikoto (Emperor Keiko) were born.
In the 30th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, when the Emperor asked each one of these two brothers what they wanted, the older brother Inishikiiribiko no mikoto wanted 'bows and arrows' and the smaller brother Otarashihiko no mikoto wanted the 'Imperial Throne,' and in this way, it was decided that Otarashihiko no mikoto would be the successor to Emperor Suinin.
In the ninth month of the 35th year of the same reign, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was dispatched to Kawachi Province and he developed many irrigation ponds and ditches, including Takashi no ike Pond and Chinu no ike Pond. It is said that he also promoted agricultural development, traveling extensively from province to province, in order to stabilize people's livelihood, and therefore, he was highly respected by the people.
In the tenth month of the 39th year of the same reign, he made one thousand swords at Utonokawakami no miya Shrine, which was possibly in the vicinity of present Jinenda, Hannan City, Osaka Prefecture). And he dedicated them to Ishigami-jinja Shrine. Emperor Suinin ordered Inishikiiribiko no mikoto to take control over the sacred treasures of the Shrine.
In the second month of the 87th year of the reign, he appointed Onakatsuhime to manage the sacred treasures for his old age. Onakatsuhime granted the sacred treasures to MONONOBE no Tochine and made him take care of them. This is said to have been the reason why the sacred treasures of Ishigami-jinja Shrine were under the custody of the Mononobe clan.
Legend about his end According to the legend handed down at Inaba-jinja Shrine in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto, once dominated Oshu Province on the Imperial edict, however, as Governor of Mutsu Province, Toyomasu, who accompanied him to Oshu, became jealous of Inishikiiribiko no mikoto's success and informed the Imperial court of a false charge that Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was plotting a treason, and thus, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was accused as an Imperial enemy and attacked to death there. When his wife, Nunoshihime no mikoto knew her husband's death, she went down to Oshu, leaving the capital, and single-mindedly longed for her late husband, devoting herself in prayer to console the spirit of the mikoto until she died. Inishikiiribiko no mikoto is highly respected even today as the shusaijin (main enshrined deities) of Inama-jinja Shrine.
For your reference, it should be noted that according to "Tensho" (a chronological history of Japan), which is said to have been a falsified record in the Edo Period, his date of birth was the 24th day of the 2nd month of the 93rd year of Emperor Suinin's reign (old lunar calendar). However in this book there is no description related to the engi (writing about the history) mentioned above.
According to the legend transmitted at Ishigami-jinja Shrine, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was living next to Ishigami-jinja Shrine, in the premises situated to the southeast of the haiden (hall of worship), and when he got senile, he wanted his younger sister Onakatsuhime to take the charge of the religious service. However, she refused his offer saying that she would not like to climb up to a higher floor (elevated bridge) because she was just a woman; then he had no idea other than appointing MONONOBE no Tochine, who was in the position of Omuraji (one of the important official ranks of Yamato Imperial Court, representing the local ruling lords called Muraji) (the religious service referred to here might have included the task of getting in and out the sacred treasures). About the chronicle of achievements mentioned above, the same episode about him is found in the article of the second month of the 87th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. It is said that he died of old age.
== Mausoleum == Udohaka Tomb is said to be his mausoleum. (it is also called Tannowa nisanzai Tomb).
It is located in Tannowa, Misaki-cho, Sennan-gun, Osaka Prefecture. It is a big-scale keyhole-shaped mound with the total length of 200m, which was constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. Although it is said to be Inishiki no mikoto's tomb, there is no evidence and it is currently under the control of the Imperial Household Agency. The nearest station is Tannowa Station on the Nankai Main Line.

According to the legend transmitted at Ishigami-jinja Shrine, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was living next to Ishigami-jinja Shrine, in the premises situated to the southeast of the haiden (hall of worship), and when he got senile, he wanted his younger sister Onakatsuhime to take the charge of the religious service. However, she refused his offer saying that she would not like to climb up to a higher floor (elevated bridge) because she was just a woman; then he had no idea other than appointing MONONOBE no Tochine, who was in the position of Omuraji (one of the important official ranks of Yamato Imperial Court, representing the local ruling lords called Muraji) (the religious service referred to here might have included the task of getting in and out the sacred treasures). About the chronicle of achievements mentioned above, the same episode about him is found in the article of the second month of the 87th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. It is said that he died of old age.
== Mausoleum == Udohaka Tomb is said to be his mausoleum. (it is also called Tannowa nisanzai Tomb).
It is located in Tannowa, Misaki-cho, Sennan-gun, Osaka Prefecture. It is a big-scale keyhole-shaped mound with the total length of 200m, which was constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. Although it is said to be Inishiki no mikoto's tomb, there is no evidence and it is currently under the control of the Imperial Household Agency. The nearest station is Tannowa Station on the Nankai Main Line.

Inishikiiribiko no mikoto (五十瓊敷入彦命) was a member of the Imperial family (Royal family), who is said to have been lived in the Kofun period (tumulus period), according to "Kojiki " (The Record of Ancient Matters) and "Nihonshoki" (Chronicles of Japan). He was the second Imperial Prince of Emperor Suinin (the first Imperial Prince was Homutsuwake no mikoto), the son born between Emperor Suinin and Sahohime). He was also mentioned as Iinishiki no mikoto (五十瓊敷命) and Inishikinoirohiko no mikoto (印色入日子命) ("Kojiki").
== Achievements Based on the chronology employed in the Nihonshoki. == Emperor Suinin had the Empress called Sahohime. After the Empress died together with her older brother Sahohiko in the rebellion provoked by himself, the Emperor took Hibasuhime as the second Empress, and the first son Inishikiiribiko no mikoto, and the second son Otarashihiko no mikoto (Emperor Keiko) were born.
In the 30th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, when the Emperor asked each one of these two brothers what they wanted, the older brother Inishikiiribiko no mikoto wanted 'bows and arrows' and the smaller brother Otarashihiko no mikoto wanted the 'Imperial Throne,' and in this way, it was decided that Otarashihiko no mikoto would be the successor to Emperor Suinin.
In the ninth month of the 35th year of the same reign, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was dispatched to Kawachi Province and he developed many irrigation ponds and ditches, including Takashi no ike Pond and Chinu no ike Pond. It is said that he also promoted agricultural development, traveling extensively from province to province, in order to stabilize people's livelihood, and therefore, he was highly respected by the people.
In the tenth month of the 39th year of the same reign, he made one thousand swords at Utonokawakami no miya Shrine, which was possibly in the vicinity of present Jinenda, Hannan City, Osaka Prefecture). And he dedicated them to Ishigami-jinja Shrine. Emperor Suinin ordered Inishikiiribiko no mikoto to take control over the sacred treasures of the Shrine.
In the second month of the 87th year of the reign, he appointed Onakatsuhime to manage the sacred treasures for his old age. Onakatsuhime granted the sacred treasures to MONONOBE no Tochine and made him take care of them. This is said to have been the reason why the sacred treasures of Ishigami-jinja Shrine were under the custody of the Mononobe clan.
Legend about his end According to the legend handed down at Inaba-jinja Shrine in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto, once dominated Oshu Province on the Imperial edict, however, as Governor of Mutsu Province, Toyomasu, who accompanied him to Oshu, became jealous of Inishikiiribiko no mikoto's success and informed the Imperial court of a false charge that Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was plotting a treason, and thus, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was accused as an Imperial enemy and attacked to death there. When his wife, Nunoshihime no mikoto knew her husband's death, she went down to Oshu, leaving the capital, and single-mindedly longed for her late husband, devoting herself in prayer to console the spirit of the mikoto until she died. Inishikiiribiko no mikoto is highly respected even today as the shusaijin (main enshrined deities) of Inama-jinja Shrine.
For your reference, it should be noted that according to "Tensho" (a chronological history of Japan), which is said to have been a falsified record in the Edo Period, his date of birth was the 24th day of the 2nd month of the 93rd year of Emperor Suinin's reign (old lunar calendar). However in this book there is no description related to the engi (writing about the history) mentioned above.
According to the legend transmitted at Ishigami-jinja Shrine, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was living next to Ishigami-jinja Shrine, in the premises situated to the southeast of the haiden (hall of worship), and when he got senile, he wanted his younger sister Onakatsuhime to take the charge of the religious service. However, she refused his offer saying that she would not like to climb up to a higher floor (elevated bridge) because she was just a woman; then he had no idea other than appointing MONONOBE no Tochine, who was in the position of Omuraji (one of the important official ranks of Yamato Imperial Court, representing the local ruling lords called Muraji) (the religious service referred to here might have included the task of getting in and out the sacred treasures). About the chronicle of achievements mentioned above, the same episode about him is found in the article of the second month of the 87th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. It is said that he died of old age.
== Mausoleum == Udohaka Tomb is said to be his mausoleum. (it is also called Tannowa nisanzai Tomb).
It is located in Tannowa, Misaki-cho, Sennan-gun, Osaka Prefecture. It is a big-scale keyhole-shaped mound with the total length of 200m, which was constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. Although it is said to be Inishiki no mikoto's tomb, there is no evidence and it is currently under the control of the Imperial Household Agency. The nearest station is Tannowa Station on the Nankai Main Line.

When his wife, Nunoshihime no mikoto knew her husband's death, she went down to Oshu, leaving the capital, and single-mindedly longed for her late husband, devoting herself in prayer to console the spirit of the mikoto until she died. Inishikiiribiko no mikoto is highly respected even today as the shusaijin (main enshrined deities) of Inama-jinja Shrine.
For your reference, it should be noted that according to "Tensho" (a chronological history of Japan), which is said to have been a falsified record in the Edo Period, his date of birth was the 24th day of the 2nd month of the 93rd year of Emperor Suinin's reign (old lunar calendar). However in this book there is no description related to the engi (writing about the history) mentioned above.
According to the legend transmitted at Ishigami-jinja Shrine, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was living next to Ishigami-jinja Shrine, in the premises situated to the southeast of the haiden (hall of worship), and when he got senile, he wanted his younger sister Onakatsuhime to take the charge of the religious service. However, she refused his offer saying that she would not like to climb up to a higher floor (elevated bridge) because she was just a woman; then he had no idea other than appointing MONONOBE no Tochine, who was in the position of Omuraji (one of the important official ranks of Yamato Imperial Court, representing the local ruling lords called Muraji) (the religious service referred to here might have included the task of getting in and out the sacred treasures). About the chronicle of achievements mentioned above, the same episode about him is found in the article of the second month of the 87th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. It is said that he died of old age.
== Mausoleum == Udohaka Tomb is said to be his mausoleum. (it is also called Tannowa nisanzai Tomb).
It is located in Tannowa, Misaki-cho, Sennan-gun, Osaka Prefecture. It is a big-scale keyhole-shaped mound with the total length of 200m, which was constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. Although it is said to be Inishiki no mikoto's tomb, there is no evidence and it is currently under the control of the Imperial Household Agency. The nearest station is Tannowa Station on the Nankai Main Line.

[[simple:user:immanuelle/Prince Inishiki]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Prince Inishiki]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Prince Inishiki]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Itsuninomikoto]] [[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Itsuninomikoto}}

Inishikiiribiko no mikoto (五十瓊敷入彦命) was a member of the Imperial family (Royal family), who is said to have been lived in the Kofun period (tumulus period), according to "Kojiki " (The Record of Ancient Matters) and "Nihonshoki" (Chronicles of Japan). He was the second Imperial Prince of Emperor Suinin (the first Imperial Prince was Homutsuwake no mikoto), the son born between Emperor Suinin and Sahohime). He was also mentioned as Iinishiki no mikoto (五十瓊敷命) and Inishikinoirohiko no mikoto (印色入日子命) ("Kojiki").
== Achievements Based on the chronology employed in the Nihonshoki. == Emperor Suinin had the Empress called Sahohime. After the Empress died together with her older brother Sahohiko in the rebellion provoked by himself, the Emperor took Hibasuhime as the second Empress, and the first son Inishikiiribiko no mikoto, and the second son Otarashihiko no mikoto (Emperor Keiko) were born.
In the 30th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, when the Emperor asked each one of these two brothers what they wanted, the older brother Inishikiiribiko no mikoto wanted 'bows and arrows' and the smaller brother Otarashihiko no mikoto wanted the 'Imperial Throne,' and in this way, it was decided that Otarashihiko no mikoto would be the successor to Emperor Suinin.
In the ninth month of the 35th year of the same reign, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was dispatched to Kawachi Province and he developed many irrigation ponds and ditches, including Takashi no ike Pond and Chinu no ike Pond. It is said that he also promoted agricultural development, traveling extensively from province to province, in order to stabilize people's livelihood, and therefore, he was highly respected by the people.
In the tenth month of the 39th year of the same reign, he made one thousand swords at Utonokawakami no miya Shrine, which was possibly in the vicinity of present Jinenda, Hannan City, Osaka Prefecture). And he dedicated them to Ishigami-jinja Shrine. Emperor Suinin ordered Inishikiiribiko no mikoto to take control over the sacred treasures of the Shrine.
In the second month of the 87th year of the reign, he appointed Onakatsuhime to manage the sacred treasures for his old age. Onakatsuhime granted the sacred treasures to MONONOBE no Tochine and made him take care of them. This is said to have been the reason why the sacred treasures of Ishigami-jinja Shrine were under the custody of the Mononobe clan.
Legend about his end According to the legend handed down at Inaba-jinja Shrine in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto, once dominated Oshu Province on the Imperial edict, however, as Governor of Mutsu Province, Toyomasu, who accompanied him to Oshu, became jealous of Inishikiiribiko no mikoto's success and informed the Imperial court of a false charge that Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was plotting a treason, and thus, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was accused as an Imperial enemy and attacked to death there. When his wife, Nunoshihime no mikoto knew her husband's death, she went down to Oshu, leaving the capital, and single-mindedly longed for her late husband, devoting herself in prayer to console the spirit of the mikoto until she died. Inishikiiribiko no mikoto is highly respected even today as the shusaijin (main enshrined deities) of Inama-jinja Shrine.
For your reference, it should be noted that according to "Tensho" (a chronological history of Japan), which is said to have been a falsified record in the Edo Period, his date of birth was the 24th day of the 2nd month of the 93rd year of Emperor Suinin's reign (old lunar calendar). However in this book there is no description related to the engi (writing about the history) mentioned above.
According to the legend transmitted at Ishigami-jinja Shrine, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was living next to Ishigami-jinja Shrine, in the premises situated to the southeast of the haiden (hall of worship), and when he got senile, he wanted his younger sister Onakatsuhime to take the charge of the religious service. However, she refused his offer saying that she would not like to climb up to a higher floor (elevated bridge) because she was just a woman; then he had no idea other than appointing MONONOBE no Tochine, who was in the position of Omuraji (one of the important official ranks of Yamato Imperial Court, representing the local ruling lords called Muraji) (the religious service referred to here might have included the task of getting in and out the sacred treasures). About the chronicle of achievements mentioned above, the same episode about him is found in the article of the second month of the 87th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. It is said that he died of old age.
== Mausoleum == Udohaka Tomb is said to be his mausoleum. (it is also called Tannowa nisanzai Tomb).
It is located in Tannowa, Misaki-cho, Sennan-gun, Osaka Prefecture. It is a big-scale keyhole-shaped mound with the total length of 200m, which was constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. Although it is said to be Inishiki no mikoto's tomb, there is no evidence and it is currently under the control of the Imperial Household Agency. The nearest station is Tannowa Station on the Nankai Main Line.

[[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|五十瓊敷入彦命伝承墓の宇度墓}}{{small|（[[大阪府]][[泉南郡]][[岬町]]の[[淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]]）}}]] '''五十瓊敷入彦命'''（いにしきいりひこのみこと{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}、生没年不詳）は、[[記紀]]に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「五十瓊敷入彦命」「五十瓊敷命」「五十瓊敷皇子」、『[[古事記]]』では「'''印色入日子命'''」と表記される。 第11代[[垂仁天皇]]の[[皇子]]で、第12代[[景行天皇]]の同母兄である。[[石上神宮]]（[[奈良県]][[天理市]]）神宝に関する伝承で知られる。 == 系譜 == {{smaller|（名称は『日本書紀』を第一とし、括弧内に『古事記』ほかを記載）}} 『[[日本書紀]]』『[[古事記]]』によれば、第11代[[垂仁天皇]]と、後皇后の[[日葉酢媛命]]（ひばすひめのみこと、比婆須比売命/氷羽州比売命）との間に生まれた[[皇子]]である。 五十瓊敷入彦命は長兄で、両書では同母弟妹として次の4人が記載される。 * 弟：大足彦尊 （おおたらしひこのみこと、大帯日子淤斯呂和気命） - 第12代'''[[景行天皇]]'''。 * 妹：[[大中姫命]] （おおなかつひめのみこと） - 記では男性の大中津日子命とする。 * 妹：[[倭姫命]] （やまとひめのみこと、倭比売命） * 弟：[[稚城瓊入彦命]] （わかきにいりひこのみこと、若木入日子命） == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』では、五十瓊敷入彦命に関して次の事績が記載されている{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 * 垂仁天皇30年1月6日条 *: [[垂仁天皇]]が五十瓊敷命・大足彦尊兄弟に望むものを聞いたところ、五十瓊敷命は弓矢を、大足彦尊は皇位を望んだ。そこで天皇は五十瓊敷命には弓矢を与え、大足彦尊には皇位を継ぐように言った{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 * 垂仁天皇35年9月条 *: 五十瓊敷命は[[河内国|河内]]に遣わされ、高石池（[[大阪府]][[高石市]]）・茅渟池（ちぬいけ：大阪府[[泉佐野市]]）を造った{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[石上神宮]]（[[奈良県]][[天理市]]）]] * 垂仁天皇39年10月条 *: 五十瓊敷命は菟砥川上宮（うとのかわかみのみや：大阪府[[泉南郡]][[阪南町]]の菟砥川流域）にて剣1千口を作り、[[石上神宮]]（[[奈良県]][[天理市]]）に納めた。そして、以後五十瓊敷命が石上神宮の神宝を管掌した{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 *: 同条別伝によると、五十瓊敷命は菟砥河上で大刀1千口を作らせ、この時に楯部・倭文部・神弓削部・神矢作部・大穴磯部・泊橿部・玉作部・神刑部・日置部・大刀佩部ら10の[[品部]]を賜った。また、その大刀は忍坂邑（奈良県[[桜井市]]忍坂）から移して石上神宮に納めたという{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 * 垂仁天皇87年2月5日条 *: 五十瓊敷命は老齢のため、石上神宮神宝の管掌を妹の大中姫命に託した。大中姫命は、か弱いことを理由に神宝を[[物部十千根]]に授けて治めさせた。これが[[物部氏]]による石上神宮での神宝管掌の起源という{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[古事記]]』垂仁天皇段では、印色入日子命（五十瓊敷入彦命）は、血沼池（茅渟池）・ 狭山池（大阪府[[大阪狭山市]]）・日下の高津池（高石池に同じか）を造ったという{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。また、鳥取河上宮にて横刀1千口を作らせて石上神宮に納めたほか、同宮にて河上部を定めたという{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 == 墓 == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|五十瓊敷入彦命 [[淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|宇度墓]]
（大阪府泉南郡岬町）}}]] [[陵墓|墓]]は、[[宮内庁]]により[[大阪府]][[泉南郡]][[岬町]][[淡輪]]にある'''宇度墓'''（うどのはか、{{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=宇度墓（五十瓊敷入彦命墓）}}）に治定されている宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}。宮内庁上の形式は[[前方後円墳|前方後円]]。遺跡名は「[[淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]]」で、墳丘長200メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。 五十瓊敷命の墓について『日本書紀』・『古事記』に記載はないが、[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵寮]]（[[諸陵式]]）では「宇度墓」の名称で記載され、[[和泉国]][[日根郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西3町・南北3町で守戸3烟を付すとしたうえで、遠墓に分類する。これに先立つ[[持統天皇]]5年（[[691年]]）には有功の王の墓には3戸の守衛戸を設けるとする詔が見えることから、この頃に『日本書紀』・『古事記』の編纂と並行して、『[[帝紀]]』や『[[旧辞]]』に基づいた墓の指定の動きがあったと推測する説がある。またその際には、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]墓（伊勢）・[[彦五瀬命]]墓（紀伊）・五十瓊敷入彦命墓（和泉）・[[菟道稚郎子]]墓（山城）をして大和国の四至を形成する意図があったとする説もある[[仁藤敦史]] 「記紀から読み解く、巨大前方後円墳の編年と問題点」『古代史研究の最前線 天皇陵』 洋泉社、2016年、pp. 13-16。。 しかし、中世には荒廃して所在が失われた{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}。[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）に『泉州志』の記載に基づき玉田山に定められたが、明治13年（[[1880年]]）に現在の墓に改められている{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}。なお、現墓について『和泉志』では[[紀小弓]]の墓としている{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}。 == 伝承 == [[伊奈波神社]]（[[岐阜県]][[岐阜市]]）の社伝によると、五十瓊敷入彦命は朝廷の詔を承けて奥州を平定したが、同行した陸奥守豊益が五十瓊敷入彦命の成功を妬んで、命に謀反の心ありと讒奏した。そのため、朝敵として攻められて同地で討たれたという。さらに、夫の死を知った妃の[[渟熨斗姫命]]（ぬのしひめのみこと：景行天皇の皇女）は、都を離れてこの地で御跡を慕い、朝夕ひたすら命の御霊を慰めつつ生涯を終えたという。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=[[上田正昭]]「五十瓊敷入彦命」|ref={{Harvid|五十瓊敷入彦命（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石井茂輔「宇度墓」（五十瓊敷入彦命項目内）|ref={{Harvid|宇度墓（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=五十瓊敷入彦命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[垂仁天皇]] * [[石上神宮]] * [[伊奈波神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:いにしきいりひこのみこと}} [[category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[五十瓊敷入彦命]] to [[Itsuninomikoto]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

（大阪府泉南郡岬町）}}]] [[陵墓|墓]]は、[[宮内庁]]により[[大阪府]][[泉南郡]][[岬町]][[淡輪]]にある'''宇度墓'''（うどのはか、{{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=宇度墓（五十瓊敷入彦命墓）}}）に治定されている宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}。宮内庁上の形式は[[前方後円墳|前方後円]]。遺跡名は「[[淡輪ニサンザイ古墳]]」で、墳丘長200メートルの[[前方後円墳]]である。 五十瓊敷命の墓について『日本書紀』・『古事記』に記載はないが、[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵寮]]（[[諸陵式]]）では「宇度墓」の名称で記載され、[[和泉国]][[日根郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西3町・南北3町で守戸3烟を付すとしたうえで、遠墓に分類する。これに先立つ[[持統天皇]]5年（[[691年]]）には有功の王の墓には3戸の守衛戸を設けるとする詔が見えることから、この頃に『日本書紀』・『古事記』の編纂と並行して、『[[帝紀]]』や『[[旧辞]]』に基づいた墓の指定の動きがあったと推測する説がある。またその際には、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]墓（伊勢）・[[彦五瀬命]]墓（紀伊）・五十瓊敷入彦命墓（和泉）・[[菟道稚郎子]]墓（山城）をして大和国の四至を形成する意図があったとする説もある[[仁藤敦史]] 「記紀から読み解く、巨大前方後円墳の編年と問題点」『古代史研究の最前線 天皇陵』 洋泉社、2016年、pp. 13-16。。 しかし、中世には荒廃して所在が失われた{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}。[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）に『泉州志』の記載に基づき玉田山に定められたが、明治13年（[[1880年]]）に現在の墓に改められている{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}。なお、現墓について『和泉志』では[[紀小弓]]の墓としている{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}。 == 伝承 == [[伊奈波神社]]（[[岐阜県]][[岐阜市]]）の社伝によると、五十瓊敷入彦命は朝廷の詔を承けて奥州を平定したが、同行した陸奥守豊益が五十瓊敷入彦命の成功を妬んで、命に謀反の心ありと讒奏した。そのため、朝敵として攻められて同地で討たれたという。さらに、夫の死を知った妃の[[渟熨斗姫命]]（ぬのしひめのみこと：景行天皇の皇女）は、都を離れてこの地で御跡を慕い、朝夕ひたすら命の御霊を慰めつつ生涯を終えたという。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=[[上田正昭]]「五十瓊敷入彦命」|ref={{Harvid|五十瓊敷入彦命（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石井茂輔「宇度墓」（五十瓊敷入彦命項目内）|ref={{Harvid|宇度墓（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=五十瓊敷入彦命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[垂仁天皇]] * [[石上神宮]] * [[伊奈波神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:いにしきいりひこのみこと}} [[category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

Inishikiiribiko no mikoto (五十瓊敷入彦命) was a member of the Imperial family (Royal family), who is said to have been lived in the Kofun period (tumulus period), according to "Kojiki " (The Record of Ancient Matters) and "Nihonshoki" (Chronicles of Japan). He was the second Imperial Prince of Emperor Suinin (the first Imperial Prince was Homutsuwake no mikoto), the son born between Emperor Suinin and Sahohime). He was also mentioned as Iinishiki no mikoto (五十瓊敷命) and Inishikinoirohiko no mikoto (印色入日子命) ("Kojiki").
== Achievements Based on the chronology employed in the Nihonshoki. == Emperor Suinin had the Empress called Sahohime. After the Empress died together with her older brother Sahohiko in the rebellion provoked by himself, the Emperor took Hibasuhime as the second Empress, and the first son Inishikiiribiko no mikoto, and the second son Otarashihiko no mikoto (Emperor Keiko) were born.
In the 30th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, when the Emperor asked each one of these two brothers what they wanted, the older brother Inishikiiribiko no mikoto wanted 'bows and arrows' and the smaller brother Otarashihiko no mikoto wanted the 'Imperial Throne,' and in this way, it was decided that Otarashihiko no mikoto would be the successor to Emperor Suinin.
In the ninth month of the 35th year of the same reign, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was dispatched to Kawachi Province and he developed many irrigation ponds and ditches, including Takashi no ike Pond and Chinu no ike Pond. It is said that he also promoted agricultural development, traveling extensively from province to province, in order to stabilize people's livelihood, and therefore, he was highly respected by the people.
In the tenth month of the 39th year of the same reign, he made one thousand swords at Utonokawakami no miya Shrine, which was possibly in the vicinity of present Jinenda, Hannan City, Osaka Prefecture). And he dedicated them to Ishigami-jinja Shrine. Emperor Suinin ordered Inishikiiribiko no mikoto to take control over the sacred treasures of the Shrine.
In the second month of the 87th year of the reign, he appointed Onakatsuhime to manage the sacred treasures for his old age. Onakatsuhime granted the sacred treasures to MONONOBE no Tochine and made him take care of them. This is said to have been the reason why the sacred treasures of Ishigami-jinja Shrine were under the custody of the Mononobe clan.
Legend about his end According to the legend handed down at Inaba-jinja Shrine in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto, once dominated Oshu Province on the Imperial edict, however, as Governor of Mutsu Province, Toyomasu, who accompanied him to Oshu, became jealous of Inishikiiribiko no mikoto's success and informed the Imperial court of a false charge that Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was plotting a treason, and thus, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was accused as an Imperial enemy and attacked to death there. When his wife, Nunoshihime no mikoto knew her husband's death, she went down to Oshu, leaving the capital, and single-mindedly longed for her late husband, devoting herself in prayer to console the spirit of the mikoto until she died. Inishikiiribiko no mikoto is highly respected even today as the shusaijin (main enshrined deities) of Inama-jinja Shrine.
For your reference, it should be noted that according to "Tensho" (a chronological history of Japan), which is said to have been a falsified record in the Edo Period, his date of birth was the 24th day of the 2nd month of the 93rd year of Emperor Suinin's reign (old lunar calendar). However in this book there is no description related to the engi (writing about the history) mentioned above.
According to the legend transmitted at Ishigami-jinja Shrine, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was living next to Ishigami-jinja Shrine, in the premises situated to the southeast of the haiden (hall of worship), and when he got senile, he wanted his younger sister Onakatsuhime to take the charge of the religious service. However, she refused his offer saying that she would not like to climb up to a higher floor (elevated bridge) because she was just a woman; then he had no idea other than appointing MONONOBE no Tochine, who was in the position of Omuraji (one of the important official Ranks of Yamato Imperial Court, representing the local ruling lords called Muraji) (the religious service referred to here might have included the task of getting in and out the sacred treasures). About the chronicle of achievements mentioned above, the same episode about him is found in the article of the second month of the 87th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. It is said that he died of old age.
== Mausoleum == Udohaka Tomb is said to be his mausoleum. (it is also called Tannowa nisanzai Tomb).
It is located in Tannowa, Misaki-cho, Sennan-gun, Osaka Prefecture. It is a big-scale keyhole-shaped mound with the total length of 200m, which was constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. Although it is said to be Inishiki no mikoto's tomb, there is no evidence and it is currently under the control of the Imperial Household Agency. The nearest station is Tannowa Station on the Nankai Main Line.

<!--Itsuninomikoto--> <!--Itsuninomikoto--> <!--Itsuninomikoto--> <!--Itsuninomikoto--> <!--Itsuninomikoto--> {{translated page|ja|五十瓊敷入彦命|version=87539929|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Inishikiiribiko no mikoto (五十瓊敷入彦命) was a member of the Imperial family (Royal family), who is said to have been lived in the Kofun period (tumulus period), according to "Kojiki " (The Record of Ancient Matters) and "Nihonshoki" (Chronicles of Japan). He was the second Imperial Prince of Emperor Suinin (the first Imperial Prince was Homutsuwake no mikoto), the son born between Emperor Suinin and Sahohime). He was also mentioned as Iinishiki no mikoto (五十瓊敷命) and Inishikinoirohiko no mikoto (印色入日子命) ("Kojiki").
== Achievements Based on the chronology employed in the Nihonshoki. == Emperor Suinin had the Empress called Sahohime. After the Empress died together with her older brother Sahohiko in the rebellion provoked by himself, the Emperor took Hibasuhime as the second Empress, and the first son Inishikiiribiko no mikoto, and the second son Otarashihiko no mikoto (Emperor Keiko) were born.
In the 30th year of Emperor Suinin's reign, when the Emperor asked each one of these two brothers what they wanted, the older brother Inishikiiribiko no mikoto wanted 'bows and arrows' and the smaller brother Otarashihiko no mikoto wanted the 'Imperial Throne,' and in this way, it was decided that Otarashihiko no mikoto would be the successor to Emperor Suinin.
In the ninth month of the 35th year of the same reign, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was dispatched to Kawachi Province and he developed many irrigation ponds and ditches, including Takashi no ike Pond and Chinu no ike Pond. It is said that he also promoted agricultural development, traveling extensively from province to province, in order to stabilize people's livelihood, and therefore, he was highly respected by the people.
In the tenth month of the 39th year of the same reign, he made one thousand swords at Utonokawakami no miya Shrine, which was possibly in the vicinity of present Jinenda, Hannan City, Osaka Prefecture). And he dedicated them to Ishigami-jinja Shrine. Emperor Suinin ordered Inishikiiribiko no mikoto to take control over the sacred treasures of the Shrine.
In the second month of the 87th year of the reign, he appointed Onakatsuhime to manage the sacred treasures for his old age. Onakatsuhime granted the sacred treasures to MONONOBE no Tochine and made him take care of them. This is said to have been the reason why the sacred treasures of Ishigami-jinja Shrine were under the custody of the Mononobe clan.
Legend about his end According to the legend handed down at Inaba-jinja Shrine in Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto, once dominated Oshu Province on the Imperial edict, however, as Governor of Mutsu Province, Toyomasu, who accompanied him to Oshu, became jealous of Inishikiiribiko no mikoto's success and informed the Imperial court of a false charge that Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was plotting a treason, and thus, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was accused as an Imperial enemy and attacked to death there. When his wife, Nunoshihime no mikoto knew her husband's death, she went down to Oshu, leaving the capital, and single-mindedly longed for her late husband, devoting herself in prayer to console the spirit of the mikoto until she died. Inishikiiribiko no mikoto is highly respected even today as the shusaijin (main enshrined deities) of Inama-jinja Shrine.
For your reference, it should be noted that according to "Tensho" (a chronological history of Japan), which is said to have been a falsified record in the Edo Period, his date of birth was the 24th day of the 2nd month of the 93rd year of Emperor Suinin's reign (old lunar calendar). However in this book there is no description related to the engi (writing about the history) mentioned above.
According to the legend transmitted at Ishigami-jinja Shrine, Inishikiiribiko no mikoto was living next to Ishigami-jinja Shrine, in the premises situated to the southeast of the haiden (hall of worship), and when he got senile, he wanted his younger sister Onakatsuhime to take the charge of the religious service. However, she refused his offer saying that she would not like to climb up to a higher floor (elevated bridge) because she was just a woman; then he had no idea other than appointing MONONOBE no Tochine, who was in the position of Omuraji (one of the important official Ranks of Yamato Imperial Court, representing the local ruling lords called Muraji) (the religious service referred to here might have included the task of getting in and out the sacred treasures). About the chronicle of achievements mentioned above, the same episode about him is found in the article of the second month of the 87th year of Emperor Suinin's reign. It is said that he died of old age.
== Mausoleum == Udohaka Tomb is said to be his mausoleum. (it is also called Tannowa nisanzai Tomb).
It is located in Tannowa, Misaki-cho, Sennan-gun, Osaka Prefecture. It is a big-scale keyhole-shaped mound with the total length of 200m, which was constructed in the latter half of the 5th century. Although it is said to be Inishiki no mikoto's tomb, there is no evidence and it is currently under the control of the Imperial Household Agency. The nearest station is Tannowa Station on the Nankai Main Line.

revision.text:
[[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Legendary Tomb (Udo no Haka) of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto}}{{small|([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]・[[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]・[[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]の{{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}) }}]] '''Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto''' (五十瓊敷入彦命{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese Imperial family member|de|Japanese Imperial family member|ja|皇族|zh|Japanese Imperial family member|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family member|qq=}} known from the {{ill|Kiki|de|Kiki|ja|記紀|zh|Kiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|qq=}} texts.
In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he is called “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”, “Inishiki no Mikoto”, or “Inishiki Ōji”, and in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as '''Inshiki Irihiko no Mikoto'''.
He was a son of the 11th [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the elder full brother of the 12th [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. He is famous in the legends concerning the sacred treasures of [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].
== Genealogy == {{smaller|(Names follow ''Nihon Shoki'' first, with ''Kojiki'' names in parentheses)}}
According to both the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was born to [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the later Empress {{ill|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|de|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|zh|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto was the eldest, and the texts record four full siblings: * Younger brother: {{ill|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|de|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大足彦尊|zh|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otari-hiko no Mikoto|qq=}} – the 12th '''[[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'''. * Younger sister: {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} (the ''Kojiki'' names the male {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大中津日子命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|qq=}}). * Younger sister: [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamato-hime no Mikoto]]. * Younger brother: {{ill|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|de|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja|稚城瓊入彦命|zh|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Records == In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', the following deeds are recorded for Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}: * Year 30, January 6 of Emperor Suinin’s reign: ** When asked what they desired, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto chose bows and arrows, and Otari-hiko no Mikoto chose the throne. The emperor granted bows and arrows to Inishiki, and told Otari-hiko to succeed to the throne{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 35, September: ** He was dispatched to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], where he excavated {{ill|Takaishi Pond|de|Takaishi Pond|ja|高石池|zh|Takaishi Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Takaishi, Osaka|Takaishi]] and {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]]] * Year 39, October: ** At the {{ill|Upper Uto River Shrine|de|Upper Uto River Shrine|ja|菟砥川上宮|zh|Upper Uto River Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Uto River Shrine|qq=}} in the {{ill|Uto River basin|de|Uto River basin|ja|菟砥川流域|zh|Uto River basin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto River basin|qq=}} (in {{ill|Hannan|de|Hannan|ja|阪南町|zh|Hannan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hannan|qq=}}, [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]), he forged 1,000 swords and dedicated them to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. From then on, he oversaw the shrine’s treasures{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. ** An alternate account says he had 1,000 great swords forged, receiving ten crafts offices (品部): {{ill|Tatebe|de|Tatebe|ja|楯部|zh|Tatebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe|qq=}}, {{ill|Worabe|de|Worabe|ja|倭文部|zh|Worabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worabe|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamiyugebe|de|Kamiyugebe|ja|神弓削部|zh|Kamiyugebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyugebe|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamiyazukuri-be|de|Kamiyazukuri-be|ja|神矢作部|zh|Kamiyazukuri-be|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyazukuri-be|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōanasobe|de|Ōanasobe|ja|大穴磯部|zh|Ōanasobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōanasobe|qq=}}, {{ill|Tomashikibe|de|Tomashikibe|ja|泊橿部|zh|Tomashikibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomashikibe|qq=}}, {{ill|Tamakurabe|de|Tamakurabe|ja|玉作部|zh|Tamakurabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakurabe|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamikōbe|de|Kamikōbe|ja|神刑部|zh|Kamikōbe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikōbe|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiokibe|de|Hiokibe|ja|日置部|zh|Hiokibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtōhaibe|de|Ōtōhaibe|ja|大刀佩部|zh|Ōtōhaibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtōhaibe|qq=}}. Those swords were moved from {{ill|Oshisaka Village|de|Oshisaka Village|ja|忍坂邑|zh|Oshisaka Village|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka Village|qq=}} to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 87, February 5: ** Due to old age, he entrusted oversight of the shrine’s treasures to his sister {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}. She, citing her delicate health, passed them to {{ill|Mononobe no Tokotsune|de|Mononobe no Tokotsune|ja|物部十千根|zh|Mononobe no Tokotsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tokotsune|qq=}}, establishing the Mononobe clan’s stewardship{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' entry for Emperor Suinin, he is credited with creating {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=}}, {{ill|Sayama Pond|de|Sayama Pond|ja|狭山池|zh|Sayama Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōsakasayama|Osakasayama]], and a pond at {{ill|Kusaka|de|Kusaka|ja|日下|zh|Kusaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka|qq=}}, and with forging 1,000 swords at {{ill|Tottori River Upper Shrine|de|Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja|鳥取河上宮|zh|Tottori River Upper Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori River Upper Shrine|qq=}} for [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. He also organized the Kawakami tribe there{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
== Tomb == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Udo no Haka (宇度墓), the tomb of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto at {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]}}]] The tomb, designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], is the '''Udo no Haka''' (宇度墓, {{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=Udo no Haka (Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto’s tomb)}}) in {{ill|Tannowa|de|Tannowa|ja|淡輪|zh|Tannowa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] called {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, 200 m long.
Although not mentioned in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927) lists “Udo no Haka” in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], {{ill|Hine District|de|Hine District|ja|日根郡|zh|Hine District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine District|qq=}}, with precincts of 3 chō east–west by 3 chō north–south, guarded by 3 households, classed as a remote tomb. An earlier 691 decree established three guard households at royal tombs of merit; scholars suggest this reflects parallel tomb-designations based on older records like the ''[[:en:Teiki|Teiki]]'' and ''[[:en:Kyūji|Kyuji]]''. One theory holds that the tombs of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoisosenu no Mikoto]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Todo Chikarako|de|Todo Chikarako|ja|菟道稚郎子|zh|Todo Chikarako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todo Chikarako|qq=}} formed the four boundaries of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]<ref>{{Harvid|仁藤}}</ref>.
The tomb fell into ruin in the medieval period{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. In 1874, based on the ''{{ill|Senshūshi|de|Senshūshi|ja|泉州志|zh|Senshūshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshūshi|qq=}}'', it was located on Mt. Tamada, but in 1880 moved to the present site{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. The ''{{ill|Izumiki|de|Izumiki|ja|和泉志|zh|Izumiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumiki|qq=}}'' records it as the tomb of {{ill|Ki no Koyumi|de|Ki no Koyumi|ja|紀小弓|zh|Ki no Koyumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Koyumi|qq=}}{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}.
== Legends == According to the shrine legend of [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]] in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto pacified Ōshū by imperial order, but Murō no Kami {{ill|Toyomasa|de|Toyomasa|ja|豊益|zh|Toyomasa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa|qq=}}, envious of his success, slandered him as rebellious. He was then attacked as a traitor and killed there. His consort, {{ill|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|de|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|zh|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Keikō, left the capital to mourn him here until her death.
== Notes ==

revision.text:
[[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Legendary Tomb (Udo no Haka) of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto}}{{small|([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]・[[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]・[[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]の{{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}) }}]] '''Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto''' (五十瓊敷入彦命{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese Imperial family member|de|Japanese Imperial family member|ja|皇族|zh|Japanese Imperial family member|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family member|qq=}} known from the {{ill|Kiki|de|Kiki|ja|記紀|zh|Kiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|qq=}} texts.
In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he is called “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”, “Inishiki no Mikoto”, or “Inishiki Ōji”, and in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as '''Inshiki Irihiko no Mikoto'''.
He was a son of the 11th [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the elder full brother of the 12th [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. He is famous in the legends concerning the sacred treasures of [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].
== Genealogy == {{smaller|(Names follow ''Nihon Shoki'' first, with ''Kojiki'' names in parentheses)}}
According to both the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was born to [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the later Empress {{ill|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|de|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|zh|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto was the eldest, and the texts record four full siblings: * Younger brother: {{ill|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|de|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大足彦尊|zh|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otari-hiko no Mikoto|qq=}} – the 12th '''[[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'''. * Younger sister: {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} (the ''Kojiki'' names the male {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大中津日子命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|qq=}}). * Younger sister: [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamato-hime no Mikoto]]. * Younger brother: {{ill|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|de|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja|稚城瓊入彦命|zh|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Records == In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', the following deeds are recorded for Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}: * Year 30, January 6 of Emperor Suinin’s reign: ** When asked what they desired, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto chose bows and arrows, and Otari-hiko no Mikoto chose the throne. The emperor granted bows and arrows to Inishiki, and told Otari-hiko to succeed to the throne{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 35, September: ** He was dispatched to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], where he excavated {{ill|Takaishi Pond|de|Takaishi Pond|ja|高石池|zh|Takaishi Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Takaishi, Osaka|Takaishi]] and {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]]] * Year 39, October: ** At the {{ill|Upper Uto River Shrine|de|Upper Uto River Shrine|ja|菟砥川上宮|zh|Upper Uto River Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Uto River Shrine|qq=}} in the {{ill|Uto River basin|de|Uto River basin|ja|菟砥川流域|zh|Uto River basin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto River basin|qq=}} (in {{ill|Hannan|de|Hannan|ja|阪南町|zh|Hannan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hannan|qq=}}, [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]), he forged 1,000 swords and dedicated them to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. From then on, he oversaw the shrine’s treasures{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. ** An alternate account says he had 1,000 great swords forged, receiving ten crafts offices (品部): {{ill|Tatebe|de|Tatebe|ja|楯部|zh|Tatebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe|qq=}}, {{ill|Worabe|de|Worabe|ja|倭文部|zh|Worabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worabe|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamiyugebe|de|Kamiyugebe|ja|神弓削部|zh|Kamiyugebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyugebe|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamiyazukuri-be|de|Kamiyazukuri-be|ja|神矢作部|zh|Kamiyazukuri-be|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyazukuri-be|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōanasobe|de|Ōanasobe|ja|大穴磯部|zh|Ōanasobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōanasobe|qq=}}, {{ill|Tomashikibe|de|Tomashikibe|ja|泊橿部|zh|Tomashikibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomashikibe|qq=}}, {{ill|Tamakurabe|de|Tamakurabe|ja|玉作部|zh|Tamakurabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakurabe|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamikōbe|de|Kamikōbe|ja|神刑部|zh|Kamikōbe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikōbe|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiokibe|de|Hiokibe|ja|日置部|zh|Hiokibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtōhaibe|de|Ōtōhaibe|ja|大刀佩部|zh|Ōtōhaibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtōhaibe|qq=}}. Those swords were moved from {{ill|Oshisaka Village|de|Oshisaka Village|ja|忍坂邑|zh|Oshisaka Village|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka Village|qq=}} to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 87, February 5: ** Due to old age, he entrusted oversight of the shrine’s treasures to his sister {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}. She, citing her delicate health, passed them to {{ill|Mononobe no Tokotsune|de|Mononobe no Tokotsune|ja|物部十千根|zh|Mononobe no Tokotsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tokotsune|qq=}}, establishing the Mononobe clan’s stewardship{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' entry for Emperor Suinin, he is credited with creating {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=}}, {{ill|Sayama Pond|de|Sayama Pond|ja|狭山池|zh|Sayama Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōsakasayama|Osakasayama]], and a pond at {{ill|Kusaka|de|Kusaka|ja|日下|zh|Kusaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka|qq=}}, and with forging 1,000 swords at {{ill|Tottori River Upper Shrine|de|Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja|鳥取河上宮|zh|Tottori River Upper Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori River Upper Shrine|qq=}} for [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. He also organized the Kawakami tribe there{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
== Tomb == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Udo no Haka (宇度墓), the tomb of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto at {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]}}]] The tomb, designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], is the '''Udo no Haka''' (宇度墓, {{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=Udo no Haka (Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto’s tomb)}}) in {{ill|Tannowa|de|Tannowa|ja|淡輪|zh|Tannowa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] called {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, 200 m long.
Although not mentioned in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927) lists “Udo no Haka” in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], {{ill|Hine District|de|Hine District|ja|日根郡|zh|Hine District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine District|qq=}}, with precincts of 3 chō east–west by 3 chō north–south, guarded by 3 households, classed as a remote tomb. An earlier 691 decree established three guard households at royal tombs of merit; scholars suggest this reflects parallel tomb-designations based on older records like the ''[[:en:Teiki|Teiki]]'' and ''[[:en:Kyūji|Kyuji]]''. One theory holds that the tombs of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoisosenu no Mikoto]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Todo Chikarako|de|Todo Chikarako|ja|菟道稚郎子|zh|Todo Chikarako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todo Chikarako|qq=}} formed the four boundaries of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]<ref>{{Harvid|仁藤}}</ref>.
The tomb fell into ruin in the medieval period{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. In 1874, based on the ''{{ill|Senshūshi|de|Senshūshi|ja|泉州志|zh|Senshūshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshūshi|qq=}}'', it was located on Mt. Tamada, but in 1880 moved to the present site{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. The ''{{ill|Izumiki|de|Izumiki|ja|和泉志|zh|Izumiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumiki|qq=}}'' records it as the tomb of {{ill|Ki no Koyumi|de|Ki no Koyumi|ja|紀小弓|zh|Ki no Koyumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Koyumi|qq=}}{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}.
== Legends == According to the shrine legend of [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]] in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto pacified Ōshū by imperial order, but Murō no Kami {{ill|Toyomasa|de|Toyomasa|ja|豊益|zh|Toyomasa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa|qq=}}, envious of his success, slandered him as rebellious. He was then attacked as a traitor and killed there. His consort, {{ill|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|de|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|zh|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Keikō, left the capital to mourn him here until her death.
== Notes ==

== References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=[[:en:Kokushi Daijiten|Kokushi Daijiten (Showa era)]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]}} ** {{Wikicite|reference={{ill|Ueda Masaaki|de|Ueda Masaaki|ja|上田正昭|zh|Ueda Masaaki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Masaaki|qq=}} “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”|ref={{Harvid|五十瓊敷入彦命（国史）}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference={{ill|Ishii Shigesuke|de|Ishii Shigesuke|ja|石井茂輔|zh|Ishii Shigesuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shigesuke|qq=}} “Udo no Haka”|ref={{Harvid|宇度墓（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|chapter=Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto (2010)|title={{ill|Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten Publishers’ Edition|de|Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten Publishers’ Edition|ja|日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|zh|Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten Publishers’ Edition|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten Publishers’ Edition|qq=}}|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588}}

revision.text:
[[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Legendary Tomb (Udo no Haka) of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto}}{{small|([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]・[[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]・[[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]の{{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}) }}]] '''Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto''' (五十瓊敷入彦命{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese Imperial family member|de|Japanese Imperial family member|ja|皇族|zh|Japanese Imperial family member|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family member|qq=}} known from the {{ill|Kiki|de|Kiki|ja|記紀|zh|Kiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|qq=}} texts.
In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he is called “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”, “Inishiki no Mikoto”, or “Inishiki Ōji”, and in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as '''Inshiki Irihiko no Mikoto'''.
He was a son of the 11th [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the elder full brother of the 12th [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. He is famous in the legends concerning the sacred treasures of [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].
== Genealogy == {{smaller|(Names follow ''Nihon Shoki'' first, with ''Kojiki'' names in parentheses)}}
According to both the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was born to [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the later Empress {{ill|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|de|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|zh|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto was the eldest, and the texts record four full siblings: * Younger brother: {{ill|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|de|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大足彦尊|zh|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otari-hiko no Mikoto|qq=}} – the 12th '''[[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'''. * Younger sister: {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} (the ''Kojiki'' names the male {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大中津日子命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|qq=}}). * Younger sister: [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamato-hime no Mikoto]]. * Younger brother: {{ill|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|de|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja|稚城瓊入彦命|zh|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Records == In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', the following deeds are recorded for Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}: * Year 30, January 6 of Emperor Suinin’s reign: ** When asked what they desired, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto chose bows and arrows, and Otari-hiko no Mikoto chose the throne. The emperor granted bows and arrows to Inishiki, and told Otari-hiko to succeed to the throne{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 35, September: ** He was dispatched to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], where he excavated {{ill|Takaishi Pond|de|Takaishi Pond|ja|高石池|zh|Takaishi Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Takaishi, Osaka|Takaishi]] and {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]]] * Year 39, October: ** At the {{ill|Upper Uto River Shrine|de|Upper Uto River Shrine|ja|菟砥川上宮|zh|Upper Uto River Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Uto River Shrine|qq=}} in the {{ill|Uto River basin|de|Uto River basin|ja|菟砥川流域|zh|Uto River basin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto River basin|qq=}} (in {{ill|Hannan|de|Hannan|ja|阪南町|zh|Hannan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hannan|qq=}}, [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]), he forged 1,000 swords and dedicated them to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. From then on, he oversaw the shrine’s treasures{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. ** An alternate account says he had 1,000 great swords forged, receiving ten crafts offices (品部): {{ill|Tatebe|de|Tatebe|ja|楯部|zh|Tatebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe|qq=}}, {{ill|Worabe|de|Worabe|ja|倭文部|zh|Worabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worabe|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamiyugebe|de|Kamiyugebe|ja|神弓削部|zh|Kamiyugebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyugebe|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamiyazukuri-be|de|Kamiyazukuri-be|ja|神矢作部|zh|Kamiyazukuri-be|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyazukuri-be|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōanasobe|de|Ōanasobe|ja|大穴磯部|zh|Ōanasobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōanasobe|qq=}}, {{ill|Tomashikibe|de|Tomashikibe|ja|泊橿部|zh|Tomashikibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomashikibe|qq=}}, {{ill|Tamakurabe|de|Tamakurabe|ja|玉作部|zh|Tamakurabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakurabe|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamikōbe|de|Kamikōbe|ja|神刑部|zh|Kamikōbe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikōbe|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiokibe|de|Hiokibe|ja|日置部|zh|Hiokibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtōhaibe|de|Ōtōhaibe|ja|大刀佩部|zh|Ōtōhaibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtōhaibe|qq=}}. Those swords were moved from {{ill|Oshisaka Village|de|Oshisaka Village|ja|忍坂邑|zh|Oshisaka Village|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka Village|qq=}} to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 87, February 5: ** Due to old age, he entrusted oversight of the shrine’s treasures to his sister {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}. She, citing her delicate health, passed them to {{ill|Mononobe no Tokotsune|de|Mononobe no Tokotsune|ja|物部十千根|zh|Mononobe no Tokotsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tokotsune|qq=}}, establishing the Mononobe clan’s stewardship{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' entry for Emperor Suinin, he is credited with creating {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=}}, {{ill|Sayama Pond|de|Sayama Pond|ja|狭山池|zh|Sayama Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōsakasayama|Osakasayama]], and a pond at {{ill|Kusaka|de|Kusaka|ja|日下|zh|Kusaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka|qq=}}, and with forging 1,000 swords at {{ill|Tottori River Upper Shrine|de|Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja|鳥取河上宮|zh|Tottori River Upper Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori River Upper Shrine|qq=}} for [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. He also organized the Kawakami tribe there{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
== Tomb == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Udo no Haka (宇度墓), the tomb of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto at {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]}}]] The tomb, designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], is the '''Udo no Haka''' (宇度墓, {{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=Udo no Haka (Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto’s tomb)}}) in {{ill|Tannowa|de|Tannowa|ja|淡輪|zh|Tannowa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] called {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, 200 m long.
Although not mentioned in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927) lists “Udo no Haka” in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], {{ill|Hine District|de|Hine District|ja|日根郡|zh|Hine District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine District|qq=}}, with precincts of 3 chō east–west by 3 chō north–south, guarded by 3 households, classed as a remote tomb. An earlier 691 decree established three guard households at royal tombs of merit; scholars suggest this reflects parallel tomb-designations based on older records like the ''[[:en:Teiki|Teiki]]'' and ''[[:en:Kyūji|Kyuji]]''. One theory holds that the tombs of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoisosenu no Mikoto]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Todo Chikarako|de|Todo Chikarako|ja|菟道稚郎子|zh|Todo Chikarako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todo Chikarako|qq=}} formed the four boundaries of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]<ref>{{Harvid|仁藤}}</ref>.
The tomb fell into ruin in the medieval period{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. In 1874, based on the ''{{ill|Senshūshi|de|Senshūshi|ja|泉州志|zh|Senshūshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshūshi|qq=}}'', it was located on Mt. Tamada, but in 1880 moved to the present site{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. The ''{{ill|Izumiki|de|Izumiki|ja|和泉志|zh|Izumiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumiki|qq=}}'' records it as the tomb of {{ill|Ki no Koyumi|de|Ki no Koyumi|ja|紀小弓|zh|Ki no Koyumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Koyumi|qq=}}{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}.
== Legends == According to the shrine legend of [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]] in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto pacified Ōshū by imperial order, but Murō no Kami {{ill|Toyomasa|de|Toyomasa|ja|豊益|zh|Toyomasa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa|qq=}}, envious of his success, slandered him as rebellious. He was then attacked as a traitor and killed there. His consort, {{ill|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|de|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|zh|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Keikō, left the capital to mourn him here until her death.
== Notes ==
