revision.text:
[[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Legendary Tomb (Udo no Haka) of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto}}{{small|([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]・[[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]・[[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]の{{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}) }}]] '''Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto''' (五十瓊敷入彦命{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese Imperial family member|de|Japanese Imperial family member|ja|皇族|zh|Japanese Imperial family member|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family member|qq=}} known from the [[:en:Kiki|Kiki]] texts.
In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he is called “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”, “Inishiki no Mikoto”, or “Inishiki Ōji”, and in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as '''Inshiki Irihiko no Mikoto'''.
He was a son of the 11th [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the elder full brother of the 12th [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. He is famous in the legends concerning the sacred treasures of [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].
== Genealogy == {{smaller|(Names follow ''Nihon Shoki'' first, with ''Kojiki'' names in parentheses)}}
According to both the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was born to [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the later Empress {{ill|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|de|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|zh|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto was the eldest, and the texts record four full siblings: * Younger brother: {{ill|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|de|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大足彦尊|zh|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otari-hiko no Mikoto|qq=}} – the 12th '''[[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'''. * Younger sister: {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} (the ''Kojiki'' names the male {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大中津日子命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|qq=}}). * Younger sister: [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamato-hime no Mikoto]]. * Younger brother: {{ill|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|de|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja|稚城瓊入彦命|zh|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Records == In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', the following deeds are recorded for Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}: * Year 30, January 6 of Emperor Suinin’s reign: ** When asked what they desired, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto chose bows and arrows, and Otari-hiko no Mikoto chose the throne. The emperor granted bows and arrows to Inishiki, and told Otari-hiko to succeed to the throne{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 35, September: ** He was dispatched to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], where he excavated {{ill|Takaishi Pond|de|Takaishi Pond|ja|高石池|zh|Takaishi Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Takaishi, Osaka|Takaishi]] and {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]]] * Year 39, October: ** At the {{ill|Upper Uto River Shrine|de|Upper Uto River Shrine|ja|菟砥川上宮|zh|Upper Uto River Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Uto River Shrine|qq=}} in the {{ill|Uto River basin|de|Uto River basin|ja|菟砥川流域|zh|Uto River basin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto River basin|qq=}} (in [[:en:Hannan|Hannan]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]), he forged 1,000 swords and dedicated them to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. From then on, he oversaw the shrine’s treasures{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. ** An alternate account says he had 1,000 great swords forged, receiving ten crafts offices (品部): {{ill|Tatebe|de|Tatebe|ja|楯部|zh|Tatebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe|qq=}}, {{ill|Worabe|de|Worabe|ja|倭文部|zh|Worabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worabe|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamiyugebe|de|Kamiyugebe|ja|神弓削部|zh|Kamiyugebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyugebe|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamiyazukuri-be|de|Kamiyazukuri-be|ja|神矢作部|zh|Kamiyazukuri-be|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyazukuri-be|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōanasobe|de|Ōanasobe|ja|大穴磯部|zh|Ōanasobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōanasobe|qq=}}, {{ill|Tomashikibe|de|Tomashikibe|ja|泊橿部|zh|Tomashikibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomashikibe|qq=}}, {{ill|Tamakurabe|de|Tamakurabe|ja|玉作部|zh|Tamakurabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakurabe|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamikōbe|de|Kamikōbe|ja|神刑部|zh|Kamikōbe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikōbe|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiokibe|de|Hiokibe|ja|日置部|zh|Hiokibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtōhaibe|de|Ōtōhaibe|ja|大刀佩部|zh|Ōtōhaibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtōhaibe|qq=}}. Those swords were moved from {{ill|Oshisaka Village|de|Oshisaka Village|ja|忍坂邑|zh|Oshisaka Village|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka Village|qq=}} to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 87, February 5: ** Due to old age, he entrusted oversight of the shrine’s treasures to his sister {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}. She, citing her delicate health, passed them to {{ill|Mononobe no Tokotsune|de|Mononobe no Tokotsune|ja|物部十千根|zh|Mononobe no Tokotsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tokotsune|qq=}}, establishing the Mononobe clan’s stewardship{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' entry for Emperor Suinin, he is credited with creating {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=}}, {{ill|Sayama Pond|de|Sayama Pond|ja|狭山池|zh|Sayama Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōsakasayama|Osakasayama]], and a pond at {{ill|Kusaka|de|Kusaka|ja|日下|zh|Kusaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka|qq=}}, and with forging 1,000 swords at {{ill|Tottori River Upper Shrine|de|Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja|鳥取河上宮|zh|Tottori River Upper Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori River Upper Shrine|qq=}} for [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. He also organized the Kawakami tribe there{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
== Tomb == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Udo no Haka (宇度墓), the tomb of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto at {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]}}]] The tomb, designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], is the '''Udo no Haka''' (宇度墓, {{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=Udo no Haka (Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto’s tomb)}}) in {{ill|Tannowa|de|Tannowa|ja|淡輪|zh|Tannowa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] called {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, 200 m long.
Although not mentioned in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927) lists “Udo no Haka” in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], {{ill|Hine District|de|Hine District|ja|日根郡|zh|Hine District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine District|qq=}}, with precincts of 3 chō east–west by 3 chō north–south, guarded by 3 households, classed as a remote tomb. An earlier 691 decree established three guard households at royal tombs of merit; scholars suggest this reflects parallel tomb-designations based on older records like the ''[[:en:Teiki|Teiki]]'' and ''[[:en:Kyūji|Kyuji]]''. One theory holds that the tombs of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoisosenu no Mikoto]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Todo Chikarako|de|Todo Chikarako|ja|菟道稚郎子|zh|Todo Chikarako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todo Chikarako|qq=}} formed the four boundaries of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]<ref>{{Harvid|仁藤}}</ref>.
The tomb fell into ruin in the medieval period{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. In 1874, based on the ''{{ill|Senshūshi|de|Senshūshi|ja|泉州志|zh|Senshūshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshūshi|qq=}}'', it was located on Mt. Tamada, but in 1880 moved to the present site{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. The ''{{ill|Izumiki|de|Izumiki|ja|和泉志|zh|Izumiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumiki|qq=}}'' records it as the tomb of {{ill|Ki no Koyumi|de|Ki no Koyumi|ja|紀小弓|zh|Ki no Koyumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Koyumi|qq=}}{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}.
== Legends == According to the shrine legend of [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]] in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto pacified Ōshū by imperial order, but Murō no Kami {{ill|Toyomasa|de|Toyomasa|ja|豊益|zh|Toyomasa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa|qq=}}, envious of his success, slandered him as rebellious. He was then attacked as a traitor and killed there. His consort, {{ill|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|de|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|zh|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Keikō, left the capital to mourn him here until her death.

[[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Legendary Tomb (Udo no Haka) of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto}}{{small|([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]・[[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]・[[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]の{{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}) }}]] '''Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto''' (五十瓊敷入彦命{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese Imperial family member|de|Japanese Imperial family member|ja|皇族|zh|Japanese Imperial family member|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family member|qq=}} known from the [[:en:Kiki|Kiki]] texts.
In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he is called “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”, “Inishiki no Mikoto”, or “Inishiki Ōji”, and in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as '''Inshiki Irihiko no Mikoto'''.
He was a son of the 11th [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the elder full brother of the 12th [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. He is famous in the legends concerning the sacred treasures of [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].
== Genealogy == {{smaller|(Names follow ''Nihon Shoki'' first, with ''Kojiki'' names in parentheses)}}
According to both the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was born to [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the later Empress {{ill|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|de|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|zh|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto was the eldest, and the texts record four full siblings: * Younger brother: {{ill|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|de|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大足彦尊|zh|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otari-hiko no Mikoto|qq=}} – the 12th '''[[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'''. * Younger sister: {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}} (the ''Kojiki'' names the male {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大中津日子命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|qq=}}). * Younger sister: [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamato-hime no Mikoto]]. * Younger brother: {{ill|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|de|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja|稚城瓊入彦命|zh|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Records == In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', the following deeds are recorded for Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}: * Year 30, January 6 of Emperor Suinin’s reign: ** When asked what they desired, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto chose bows and arrows, and Otari-hiko no Mikoto chose the throne. The emperor granted bows and arrows to Inishiki, and told Otari-hiko to succeed to the throne{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 35, September: ** He was dispatched to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], where he excavated {{ill|Takaishi Pond|de|Takaishi Pond|ja|高石池|zh|Takaishi Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Takaishi, Osaka|Takaishi]] and {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]]] * Year 39, October: ** At the {{ill|Upper Uto River Shrine|de|Upper Uto River Shrine|ja|菟砥川上宮|zh|Upper Uto River Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Uto River Shrine|qq=}} in the {{ill|Uto River basin|de|Uto River basin|ja|菟砥川流域|zh|Uto River basin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto River basin|qq=}} (in [[:en:Hannan|Hannan]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]), he forged 1,000 swords and dedicated them to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. From then on, he oversaw the shrine’s treasures{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. ** An alternate account says he had 1,000 great swords forged, receiving ten crafts offices (品部): {{ill|Tatebe|de|Tatebe|ja|楯部|zh|Tatebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe|qq=}}, {{ill|Worabe|de|Worabe|ja|倭文部|zh|Worabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worabe|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamiyugebe|de|Kamiyugebe|ja|神弓削部|zh|Kamiyugebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyugebe|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamiyazukuri-be|de|Kamiyazukuri-be|ja|神矢作部|zh|Kamiyazukuri-be|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyazukuri-be|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōanasobe|de|Ōanasobe|ja|大穴磯部|zh|Ōanasobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōanasobe|qq=}}, {{ill|Tomashikibe|de|Tomashikibe|ja|泊橿部|zh|Tomashikibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomashikibe|qq=}}, {{ill|Tamakurabe|de|Tamakurabe|ja|玉作部|zh|Tamakurabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakurabe|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamikōbe|de|Kamikōbe|ja|神刑部|zh|Kamikōbe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikōbe|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiokibe|de|Hiokibe|ja|日置部|zh|Hiokibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtōhaibe|de|Ōtōhaibe|ja|大刀佩部|zh|Ōtōhaibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtōhaibe|qq=}}. Those swords were moved from {{ill|Oshisaka Village|de|Oshisaka Village|ja|忍坂邑|zh|Oshisaka Village|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka Village|qq=}} to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 87, February 5: ** Due to old age, he entrusted oversight of the shrine’s treasures to his sister {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}. She, citing her delicate health, passed them to {{ill|Mononobe no Tokotsune|de|Mononobe no Tokotsune|ja|物部十千根|zh|Mononobe no Tokotsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tokotsune|qq=}}, establishing the Mononobe clan’s stewardship{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' entry for Emperor Suinin, he is credited with creating {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=}}, {{ill|Sayama Pond|de|Sayama Pond|ja|狭山池|zh|Sayama Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōsakasayama|Osakasayama]], and a pond at {{ill|Kusaka|de|Kusaka|ja|日下|zh|Kusaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka|qq=}}, and with forging 1,000 swords at {{ill|Tottori River Upper Shrine|de|Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja|鳥取河上宮|zh|Tottori River Upper Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori River Upper Shrine|qq=}} for [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. He also organized the Kawakami tribe there{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
== Tomb == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Udo no Haka (宇度墓), the tomb of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto at {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]}}]] The tomb, designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], is the '''Udo no Haka''' (宇度墓, {{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=Udo no Haka (Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto’s tomb)}}) in {{ill|Tannowa|de|Tannowa|ja|淡輪|zh|Tannowa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] called {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, 200 m long.
Although not mentioned in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927) lists “Udo no Haka” in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], {{ill|Hine District|de|Hine District|ja|日根郡|zh|Hine District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine District|qq=}}, with precincts of 3 chō east–west by 3 chō north–south, guarded by 3 households, classed as a remote tomb. An earlier 691 decree established three guard households at royal tombs of merit; scholars suggest this reflects parallel tomb-designations based on older records like the ''[[:en:Teiki|Teiki]]'' and ''[[:en:Kyūji|Kyuji]]''. One theory holds that the tombs of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoisosenu no Mikoto]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Todo Chikarako|de|Todo Chikarako|ja|菟道稚郎子|zh|Todo Chikarako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todo Chikarako|qq=}} formed the four boundaries of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]<ref>{{Harvid|仁藤}}</ref>.
The tomb fell into ruin in the medieval period{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. In 1874, based on the ''{{ill|Senshūshi|de|Senshūshi|ja|泉州志|zh|Senshūshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshūshi|qq=}}'', it was located on Mt. Tamada, but in 1880 moved to the present site{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. The ''{{ill|Izumiki|de|Izumiki|ja|和泉志|zh|Izumiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumiki|qq=}}'' records it as the tomb of {{ill|Ki no Koyumi|de|Ki no Koyumi|ja|紀小弓|zh|Ki no Koyumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Koyumi|qq=}}{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}.
== Legends == According to the shrine legend of [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]] in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto pacified Ōshū by imperial order, but Murō no Kami {{ill|Toyomasa|de|Toyomasa|ja|豊益|zh|Toyomasa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa|qq=}}, envious of his success, slandered him as rebellious. He was then attacked as a traitor and killed there. His consort, {{ill|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|de|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|zh|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Keikō, left the capital to mourn him here until her death.

[[simple:user:immanuelle/Prince Inishiki]] [[sn:Prince Inishikiirihiko]] [[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] {{Isonokami Faith}} <!--Prince Inishikiirihiko--><!--Prince Inishikiirihiko--><!--Prince Inishikiirihiko--><!--Prince Inishikiirihiko--><!--Prince Inishikiirihiko--> [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

[[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Legendary Tomb (Udo no Haka) of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto}}{{small|([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]・[[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]・[[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]の{{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}) }}]] '''Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto''' (五十瓊敷入彦命{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese Imperial family member|de|Japanese Imperial family member|ja|皇族|zh|Japanese Imperial family member|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family member|qq=}} known from the [[:en:Kiki|Kiki]] texts.
In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he is called “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”, “Inishiki no Mikoto”, or “Inishiki Ōji”, and in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as '''Inshiki Irihiko no Mikoto'''.
He was a son of the 11th [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the elder full brother of the 12th [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. He is famous in the legends concerning the sacred treasures of [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].
== Genealogy == {{smaller|(Names follow ''Nihon Shoki'' first, with ''Kojiki'' names in parentheses)}}
According to both the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was born to [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the later Empress {{ill|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|de|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|zh|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto was the eldest, and the texts record four full siblings: * Younger brother: {{ill|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|de|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大足彦尊|zh|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otari-hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the 12th '''[[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'''. * Younger sister: {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the ''Kojiki'' names the male {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大中津日子命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Younger sister: [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamato-hime no Mikoto]]. * Younger brother: {{ill|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|de|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja|稚城瓊入彦命|zh|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Records == In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', the following deeds are recorded for Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}: * Year 30, January 6 of Emperor Suinin’s reign: ** When asked what they desired, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto chose bows and arrows, and Otari-hiko no Mikoto chose the throne. The emperor granted bows and arrows to Inishiki, and told Otari-hiko to succeed to the throne{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 35, September: ** He was dispatched to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], where he excavated {{ill|Takaishi Pond|de|Takaishi Pond|ja|高石池|zh|Takaishi Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi Pond|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Takaishi, Osaka|Takaishi]] and {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]]] * Year 39, October: ** At the {{ill|Upper Uto River Shrine|de|Upper Uto River Shrine|ja|菟砥川上宮|zh|Upper Uto River Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Uto River Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Uto River basin|de|Uto River basin|ja|菟砥川流域|zh|Uto River basin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto River basin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (in [[:en:Hannan|Hannan]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]), he forged 1,000 swords and dedicated them to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. From then on, he oversaw the shrine’s treasures{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. ** An alternate account says he had 1,000 great swords forged, receiving ten crafts offices (品部): {{ill|Tatebe|de|Tatebe|ja|楯部|zh|Tatebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Worabe|de|Worabe|ja|倭文部|zh|Worabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worabe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyugebe|de|Kamiyugebe|ja|神弓削部|zh|Kamiyugebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyugebe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyazukuri-be|de|Kamiyazukuri-be|ja|神矢作部|zh|Kamiyazukuri-be|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyazukuri-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōanasobe|de|Ōanasobe|ja|大穴磯部|zh|Ōanasobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōanasobe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tomashikibe|de|Tomashikibe|ja|泊橿部|zh|Tomashikibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomashikibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tamakurabe|de|Tamakurabe|ja|玉作部|zh|Tamakurabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakurabe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamikōbe|de|Kamikōbe|ja|神刑部|zh|Kamikōbe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikōbe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hiokibe|de|Hiokibe|ja|日置部|zh|Hiokibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōtōhaibe|de|Ōtōhaibe|ja|大刀佩部|zh|Ōtōhaibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtōhaibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Those swords were moved from {{ill|Oshisaka Village|de|Oshisaka Village|ja|忍坂邑|zh|Oshisaka Village|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 87, February 5: ** Due to old age, he entrusted oversight of the shrine’s treasures to his sister {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She, citing her delicate health, passed them to {{ill|Mononobe no Tokotsune|de|Mononobe no Tokotsune|ja|物部十千根|zh|Mononobe no Tokotsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tokotsune|qq=}}, establishing the Mononobe clan’s stewardship{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' entry for Emperor Suinin, he is credited with creating {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sayama Pond|de|Sayama Pond|ja|狭山池|zh|Sayama Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōsakasayama|Osakasayama]], and a pond at {{ill|Kusaka|de|Kusaka|ja|日下|zh|Kusaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and with forging 1,000 swords at {{ill|Tottori River Upper Shrine|de|Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja|鳥取河上宮|zh|Tottori River Upper Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori River Upper Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. He also organized the Kawakami tribe there{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
== Tomb == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Udo no Haka (宇度墓), the tomb of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto at {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]}}]] The tomb, designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], is the '''Udo no Haka''' (宇度墓, {{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=Udo no Haka (Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto’s tomb)}}) in {{ill|Tannowa|de|Tannowa|ja|淡輪|zh|Tannowa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] called {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, 200 m long.
Although not mentioned in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927) lists “Udo no Haka” in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], {{ill|Hine District|de|Hine District|ja|日根郡|zh|Hine District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine District|qq=}}, with precincts of 3 chō east–west by 3 chō north–south, guarded by 3 households, classed as a remote tomb. An earlier 691 decree established three guard households at royal tombs of merit; scholars suggest this reflects parallel tomb-designations based on older records like the ''[[:en:Teiki|Teiki]]'' and ''[[:en:Kyūji|Kyuji]]''. One theory holds that the tombs of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoisosenu no Mikoto]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Todo Chikarako|de|Todo Chikarako|ja|菟道稚郎子|zh|Todo Chikarako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todo Chikarako|qq=}} formed the four boundaries of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]<ref>{{Harvid|仁藤}}</ref>.
The tomb fell into ruin in the medieval period{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. In 1874, based on the ''{{ill|Senshūshi|de|Senshūshi|ja|泉州志|zh|Senshūshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshūshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', it was located on Mt. Tamada, but in 1880 moved to the present site{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. The ''{{ill|Izumiki|de|Izumiki|ja|和泉志|zh|Izumiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'' records it as the tomb of {{ill|Ki no Koyumi|de|Ki no Koyumi|ja|紀小弓|zh|Ki no Koyumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Koyumi|qq=}}{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}.
== Legends == According to the shrine legend of [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]] in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto pacified Ōshū by imperial order, but Murō no Kami {{ill|Toyomasa|de|Toyomasa|ja|豊益|zh|Toyomasa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, envious of his success, slandered him as rebellious. He was then attacked as a traitor and killed there. His consort, {{ill|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|de|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|zh|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Keikō, left the capital to mourn him here until her death.

[[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Legendary Tomb (Udo no Haka) of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto}}{{small|([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]・[[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]・[[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]の{{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}) }}]] '''Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto''' (五十瓊敷入彦命{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese Imperial family member|de|Japanese Imperial family member|ja|皇族|zh|Japanese Imperial family member|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family member|qq=}} known from the [[:en:Kiki|Kiki]] texts.
In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he is called “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”, “Inishiki no Mikoto”, or “Inishiki Ōji”, and in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as '''Inshiki Irihiko no Mikoto'''.
He was a son of the 11th [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the elder full brother of the 12th [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. He is famous in the legends concerning the sacred treasures of [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].
== Genealogy == {{smaller|(Names follow ''Nihon Shoki'' first, with ''Kojiki'' names in parentheses)}}
According to both the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was born to [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the later Empress {{ill|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|de|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|zh|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto was the eldest, and the texts record four full siblings: * Younger brother: {{ill|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|de|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大足彦尊|zh|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otari-hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the 12th '''[[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'''. * Younger sister: {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the ''Kojiki'' names the male {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大中津日子命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Younger sister: [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamato-hime no Mikoto]]. * Younger brother: {{ill|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|de|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja|稚城瓊入彦命|zh|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Records == In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', the following deeds are recorded for Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]: * Year 30, January 6 of Emperor Suinin’s reign: ** When asked what they desired, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto chose bows and arrows, and Otari-hiko no Mikoto chose the throne. The emperor granted bows and arrows to Inishiki, and told Otari-hiko to succeed to the throne{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. * Year 35, September: ** He was dispatched to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], where he excavated {{ill|Takaishi Pond|de|Takaishi Pond|ja|高石池|zh|Takaishi Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi Pond|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Takaishi, Osaka|Takaishi]] and {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]]] * Year 39, October: ** At the {{ill|Upper Uto River Shrine|de|Upper Uto River Shrine|ja|菟砥川上宮|zh|Upper Uto River Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Uto River Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Uto River basin|de|Uto River basin|ja|菟砥川流域|zh|Uto River basin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto River basin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (in [[:en:Hannan|Hannan]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]), he forged 1,000 swords and dedicated them to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. From then on, he oversaw the shrine’s treasures{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. ** An alternate account says he had 1,000 great swords forged, receiving ten crafts offices (品部): {{ill|Tatebe|de|Tatebe|ja|楯部|zh|Tatebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Worabe|de|Worabe|ja|倭文部|zh|Worabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worabe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyugebe|de|Kamiyugebe|ja|神弓削部|zh|Kamiyugebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyugebe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyazukuri-be|de|Kamiyazukuri-be|ja|神矢作部|zh|Kamiyazukuri-be|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyazukuri-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōanasobe|de|Ōanasobe|ja|大穴磯部|zh|Ōanasobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōanasobe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tomashikibe|de|Tomashikibe|ja|泊橿部|zh|Tomashikibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomashikibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tamakurabe|de|Tamakurabe|ja|玉作部|zh|Tamakurabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakurabe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamikōbe|de|Kamikōbe|ja|神刑部|zh|Kamikōbe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikōbe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hiokibe|de|Hiokibe|ja|日置部|zh|Hiokibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōtōhaibe|de|Ōtōhaibe|ja|大刀佩部|zh|Ōtōhaibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtōhaibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Those swords were moved from {{ill|Oshisaka Village|de|Oshisaka Village|ja|忍坂邑|zh|Oshisaka Village|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. * Year 87, February 5: ** Due to old age, he entrusted oversight of the shrine’s treasures to his sister {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She, citing her delicate health, passed them to {{ill|Mononobe no Tokotsune|de|Mononobe no Tokotsune|ja|物部十千根|zh|Mononobe no Tokotsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tokotsune|qq=}}, establishing the Mononobe clan’s stewardship{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]].
In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' entry for Emperor Suinin, he is credited with creating {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sayama Pond|de|Sayama Pond|ja|狭山池|zh|Sayama Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōsakasayama|Osakasayama]], and a pond at {{ill|Kusaka|de|Kusaka|ja|日下|zh|Kusaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and with forging 1,000 swords at {{ill|Tottori River Upper Shrine|de|Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja|鳥取河上宮|zh|Tottori River Upper Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori River Upper Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. He also organized the Kawakami tribe there{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]].
== Tomb == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Udo no Haka (宇度墓), the tomb of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto at {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]}}]] The tomb, designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], is the '''Udo no Haka''' (宇度墓, {{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=Udo no Haka (Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto’s tomb)}}) in {{ill|Tannowa|de|Tannowa|ja|淡輪|zh|Tannowa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] called {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, 200 m long.
Although not mentioned in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927) lists “Udo no Haka” in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], {{ill|Hine District|de|Hine District|ja|日根郡|zh|Hine District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine District|qq=}}, with precincts of 3 chō east–west by 3 chō north–south, guarded by 3 households, classed as a remote tomb. An earlier 691 decree established three guard households at royal tombs of merit; scholars suggest this reflects parallel tomb-designations based on older records like the ''[[:en:Teiki|Teiki]]'' and ''[[:en:Kyūji|Kyuji]]''. One theory holds that the tombs of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoisosenu no Mikoto]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Todo Chikarako|de|Todo Chikarako|ja|菟道稚郎子|zh|Todo Chikarako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todo Chikarako|qq=}} formed the four boundaries of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]<ref>{{Harvid|仁藤}}</ref>.
The tomb fell into ruin in the medieval period{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. In 1874, based on the ''{{ill|Senshūshi|de|Senshūshi|ja|泉州志|zh|Senshūshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshūshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', it was located on Mt. Tamada, but in 1880 moved to the present site{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. The ''{{ill|Izumiki|de|Izumiki|ja|和泉志|zh|Izumiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'' records it as the tomb of {{ill|Ki no Koyumi|de|Ki no Koyumi|ja|紀小弓|zh|Ki no Koyumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Koyumi|qq=}}{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}.
== Legends == According to the shrine legend of [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]] in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto pacified Ōshū by imperial order, but Murō no Kami {{ill|Toyomasa|de|Toyomasa|ja|豊益|zh|Toyomasa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, envious of his success, slandered him as rebellious. He was then attacked as a traitor and killed there. His consort, {{ill|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|de|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|zh|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Keikō, left the capital to mourn him here until her death.

[[simple:user:immanuelle/Prince Inishiki]] [[sn:Prince Inishikiirihiko]] [[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] {{Isonokami Faith}} <!--Prince Inishikiirihiko--><!--Prince Inishikiirihiko--><!--Prince Inishikiirihiko--><!--Prince Inishikiirihiko--><!--Prince Inishikiirihiko--> [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

[[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Legendary Tomb (Udo no Haka) of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto}}{{small|([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]・[[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]・[[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]の{{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}) }}]] '''Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto''' (五十瓊敷入彦命{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese Imperial family member|de|Japanese Imperial family member|ja|皇族|zh|Japanese Imperial family member|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family member|qq=}} known from the [[:en:Kiki|Kiki]] texts.
In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he is called “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”, “Inishiki no Mikoto”, or “Inishiki Ōji”, and in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as '''Inshiki Irihiko no Mikoto'''.
He was a son of the 11th [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the elder full brother of the 12th [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. He is famous in the legends concerning the sacred treasures of [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].
== Genealogy == {{smaller|(Names follow ''Nihon Shoki'' first, with ''Kojiki'' names in parentheses)}}
According to both the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was born to [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the later Empress {{ill|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|de|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|zh|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto was the eldest, and the texts record four full siblings: * Younger brother: {{ill|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|de|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大足彦尊|zh|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otari-hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the 12th '''[[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'''. * Younger sister: {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the ''Kojiki'' names the male {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大中津日子命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Younger sister: [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamato-hime no Mikoto]]. * Younger brother: {{ill|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|de|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja|稚城瓊入彦命|zh|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Records == In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', the following deeds are recorded for Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]: * Year 30, January 6 of Emperor Suinin’s reign: ** When asked what they desired, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto chose bows and arrows, and Otari-hiko no Mikoto chose the throne. The emperor granted bows and arrows to Inishiki, and told Otari-hiko to succeed to the throne{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. * Year 35, September: ** He was dispatched to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], where he excavated {{ill|Takaishi Pond|de|Takaishi Pond|ja|高石池|zh|Takaishi Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi Pond|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Takaishi, Osaka|Takaishi]] and {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]]] * Year 39, October: ** At the {{ill|Upper Uto River Shrine|de|Upper Uto River Shrine|ja|菟砥川上宮|zh|Upper Uto River Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Uto River Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Uto River basin|de|Uto River basin|ja|菟砥川流域|zh|Uto River basin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto River basin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (in [[:en:Hannan|Hannan]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]), he forged 1,000 swords and dedicated them to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. From then on, he oversaw the shrine’s treasures{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. ** An alternate account says he had 1,000 great swords forged, receiving ten crafts offices (品部): {{ill|Tatebe|de|Tatebe|ja|楯部|zh|Tatebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Worabe|de|Worabe|ja|倭文部|zh|Worabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worabe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyugebe|de|Kamiyugebe|ja|神弓削部|zh|Kamiyugebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyugebe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyazukuri-be|de|Kamiyazukuri-be|ja|神矢作部|zh|Kamiyazukuri-be|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyazukuri-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōanasobe|de|Ōanasobe|ja|大穴磯部|zh|Ōanasobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōanasobe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tomashikibe|de|Tomashikibe|ja|泊橿部|zh|Tomashikibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomashikibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tamakurabe|de|Tamakurabe|ja|玉作部|zh|Tamakurabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakurabe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamikōbe|de|Kamikōbe|ja|神刑部|zh|Kamikōbe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikōbe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hiokibe|de|Hiokibe|ja|日置部|zh|Hiokibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōtōhaibe|de|Ōtōhaibe|ja|大刀佩部|zh|Ōtōhaibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtōhaibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Those swords were moved from {{ill|Oshisaka Village|de|Oshisaka Village|ja|忍坂邑|zh|Oshisaka Village|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. * Year 87, February 5: ** Due to old age, he entrusted oversight of the shrine’s treasures to his sister {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She, citing her delicate health, passed them to {{ill|Mononobe no Tokotsune|de|Mononobe no Tokotsune|ja|物部十千根|zh|Mononobe no Tokotsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tokotsune|qq=}}, establishing the Mononobe clan’s stewardship{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]].
In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' entry for Emperor Suinin, he is credited with creating {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sayama Pond|de|Sayama Pond|ja|狭山池|zh|Sayama Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōsakasayama|Osakasayama]], and a pond at {{ill|Kusaka|de|Kusaka|ja|日下|zh|Kusaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and with forging 1,000 swords at {{ill|Tottori River Upper Shrine|de|Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja|鳥取河上宮|zh|Tottori River Upper Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori River Upper Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. He also organized the Kawakami tribe there{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]].
== Tomb == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Udo no Haka (宇度墓), the tomb of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto at {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]}}]] The tomb, designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], is the '''Udo no Haka''' (宇度墓, {{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=Udo no Haka (Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto’s tomb)}}) in {{ill|Tannowa|de|Tannowa|ja|淡輪|zh|Tannowa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] called {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, 200 m long.
Although not mentioned in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927) lists “Udo no Haka” in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], {{ill|Hine District|de|Hine District|ja|日根郡|zh|Hine District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine District|qq=}}, with precincts of 3 chō east–west by 3 chō north–south, guarded by 3 households, classed as a remote tomb. An earlier 691 decree established three guard households at royal tombs of merit; scholars suggest this reflects parallel tomb-designations based on older records like the ''[[:en:Teiki|Teiki]]'' and ''[[:en:Kyūji|Kyuji]]''. One theory holds that the tombs of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoisosenu no Mikoto]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Todo Chikarako|de|Todo Chikarako|ja|菟道稚郎子|zh|Todo Chikarako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todo Chikarako|qq=}} formed the four boundaries of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]<ref>{{Harvid|仁藤}}</ref>.
The tomb fell into ruin in the medieval period{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. In 1874, based on the ''{{ill|Senshūshi|de|Senshūshi|ja|泉州志|zh|Senshūshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshūshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', it was located on Mt. Tamada, but in 1880 moved to the present site{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. The ''{{ill|Izumiki|de|Izumiki|ja|和泉志|zh|Izumiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'' records it as the tomb of {{ill|Ki no Koyumi|de|Ki no Koyumi|ja|紀小弓|zh|Ki no Koyumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Koyumi|qq=}}{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}.
== Legends == According to the shrine legend of [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]] in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto pacified Ōshū by imperial order, but Murō no Kami {{ill|Toyomasa|de|Toyomasa|ja|豊益|zh|Toyomasa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, envious of his success, slandered him as rebellious. He was then attacked as a traitor and killed there. His consort, {{ill|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|de|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|zh|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Keikō, left the capital to mourn him here until her death.
== Notes ==

revision.text:
[[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Legendary Tomb (Udo no Haka) of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto}}{{small|([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]・[[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]・[[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]の{{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}) }}]] '''Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto''' (五十瓊敷入彦命{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese Imperial family member|de|Japanese Imperial family member|ja|皇族|zh|Japanese Imperial family member|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family member|qq=}} known from the [[:en:Kiki|Kiki]] texts.
In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he is called “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”, “Inishiki no Mikoto”, or “Inishiki Ōji”, and in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as '''Inshiki Irihiko no Mikoto'''.
He was a son of the 11th [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the elder full brother of the 12th [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. He is famous in the legends concerning the sacred treasures of [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].
== Genealogy == {{smaller|(Names follow ''Nihon Shoki'' first, with ''Kojiki'' names in parentheses)}}
According to both the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was born to [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the later Empress {{ill|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|de|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|zh|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto was the eldest, and the texts record four full siblings: * Younger brother: {{ill|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|de|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大足彦尊|zh|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otari-hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the 12th '''[[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'''. * Younger sister: {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the ''Kojiki'' names the male {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大中津日子命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Younger sister: [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamato-hime no Mikoto]]. * Younger brother: {{ill|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|de|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja|稚城瓊入彦命|zh|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Records == In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', the following deeds are recorded for Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]: * Year 30, January 6 of Emperor Suinin’s reign: ** When asked what they desired, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto chose bows and arrows, and Otari-hiko no Mikoto chose the throne. The emperor granted bows and arrows to Inishiki, and told Otari-hiko to succeed to the throne{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. * Year 35, September: ** He was dispatched to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], where he excavated {{ill|Takaishi Pond|de|Takaishi Pond|ja|高石池|zh|Takaishi Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi Pond|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Takaishi, Osaka|Takaishi]] and {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]]] * Year 39, October: ** At the {{ill|Upper Uto River Shrine|de|Upper Uto River Shrine|ja|菟砥川上宮|zh|Upper Uto River Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Uto River Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Uto River basin|de|Uto River basin|ja|菟砥川流域|zh|Uto River basin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto River basin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (in [[:en:Hannan|Hannan]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]), he forged 1,000 swords and dedicated them to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. From then on, he oversaw the shrine’s treasures{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. ** An alternate account says he had 1,000 great swords forged, receiving ten crafts offices (品部): {{ill|Tatebe|de|Tatebe|ja|楯部|zh|Tatebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Worabe|de|Worabe|ja|倭文部|zh|Worabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worabe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyugebe|de|Kamiyugebe|ja|神弓削部|zh|Kamiyugebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyugebe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyazukuri-be|de|Kamiyazukuri-be|ja|神矢作部|zh|Kamiyazukuri-be|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyazukuri-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōanasobe|de|Ōanasobe|ja|大穴磯部|zh|Ōanasobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōanasobe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tomashikibe|de|Tomashikibe|ja|泊橿部|zh|Tomashikibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomashikibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tamakurabe|de|Tamakurabe|ja|玉作部|zh|Tamakurabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakurabe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamikōbe|de|Kamikōbe|ja|神刑部|zh|Kamikōbe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikōbe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hiokibe|de|Hiokibe|ja|日置部|zh|Hiokibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōtōhaibe|de|Ōtōhaibe|ja|大刀佩部|zh|Ōtōhaibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtōhaibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Those swords were moved from {{ill|Oshisaka Village|de|Oshisaka Village|ja|忍坂邑|zh|Oshisaka Village|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. * Year 87, February 5: ** Due to old age, he entrusted oversight of the shrine’s treasures to his sister {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She, citing her delicate health, passed them to {{ill|Mononobe no Tokotsune|de|Mononobe no Tokotsune|ja|物部十千根|zh|Mononobe no Tokotsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tokotsune|qq=}}, establishing the Mononobe clan’s stewardship{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]].
In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' entry for Emperor Suinin, he is credited with creating {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sayama Pond|de|Sayama Pond|ja|狭山池|zh|Sayama Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōsakasayama|Osakasayama]], and a pond at {{ill|Kusaka|de|Kusaka|ja|日下|zh|Kusaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and with forging 1,000 swords at {{ill|Tottori River Upper Shrine|de|Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja|鳥取河上宮|zh|Tottori River Upper Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori River Upper Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. He also organized the Kawakami tribe there{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]].
== Tomb == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Udo no Haka (宇度墓), the tomb of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto at {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]}}]] The tomb, designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], is the '''Udo no Haka''' (宇度墓, {{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=Udo no Haka (Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto’s tomb)}}) in {{ill|Tannowa|de|Tannowa|ja|淡輪|zh|Tannowa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] called {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, 200 m long.
Although not mentioned in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927) lists “Udo no Haka” in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], {{ill|Hine District|de|Hine District|ja|日根郡|zh|Hine District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine District|qq=}}, with precincts of 3 chō east–west by 3 chō north–south, guarded by 3 households, classed as a remote tomb. An earlier 691 decree established three guard households at royal tombs of merit; scholars suggest this reflects parallel tomb-designations based on older records like the ''[[:en:Teiki|Teiki]]'' and ''[[:en:Kyūji|Kyuji]]''. One theory holds that the tombs of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoisosenu no Mikoto]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Todo Chikarako|de|Todo Chikarako|ja|菟道稚郎子|zh|Todo Chikarako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todo Chikarako|qq=}} formed the four boundaries of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]<ref>{{Harvid|仁藤}}</ref>.
The tomb fell into ruin in the medieval period{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. In 1874, based on the ''{{ill|Senshūshi|de|Senshūshi|ja|泉州志|zh|Senshūshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshūshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', it was located on Mt. Tamada, but in 1880 moved to the present site{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. The ''{{ill|Izumiki|de|Izumiki|ja|和泉志|zh|Izumiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'' records it as the tomb of {{ill|Ki no Koyumi|de|Ki no Koyumi|ja|紀小弓|zh|Ki no Koyumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Koyumi|qq=}}{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}.
== Legends == According to the shrine legend of [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]] in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto pacified Ōshū by imperial order, but Murō no Kami {{ill|Toyomasa|de|Toyomasa|ja|豊益|zh|Toyomasa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, envious of his success, slandered him as rebellious. He was then attacked as a traitor and killed there. His consort, {{ill|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|de|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|zh|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Keikō, left the capital to mourn him here until her death.

revision.text:
[[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Legendary Tomb (Udo no Haka) of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto}}{{small|([[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]・[[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]・[[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]の{{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}) }}]] '''Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto''' (五十瓊敷入彦命{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese Imperial family member|de|Japanese Imperial family member|ja|皇族|zh|Japanese Imperial family member|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family member|qq=}} known from the [[:en:Kiki|Kiki]] texts.
In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', he is called “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”, “Inishiki no Mikoto”, or “Inishiki Ōji”, and in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as '''Inshiki Irihiko no Mikoto'''.
He was a son of the 11th [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the elder full brother of the 12th [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. He is famous in the legends concerning the sacred treasures of [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].
== Genealogy == {{smaller|(Names follow ''Nihon Shoki'' first, with ''Kojiki'' names in parentheses)}}
According to both the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was born to [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the later Empress {{ill|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|de|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|zh|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}.
Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto was the eldest, and the texts record four full siblings: * Younger brother: {{ill|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|de|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大足彦尊|zh|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otari-hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the 12th '''[[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'''. * Younger sister: {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the ''Kojiki'' names the male {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大中津日子命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Younger sister: [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamato-hime no Mikoto]]. * Younger brother: {{ill|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|de|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja|稚城瓊入彦命|zh|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Records == In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', the following deeds are recorded for Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]: * Year 30, January 6 of Emperor Suinin’s reign: ** When asked what they desired, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto chose bows and arrows, and Otari-hiko no Mikoto chose the throne. The emperor granted bows and arrows to Inishiki, and told Otari-hiko to succeed to the throne{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. * Year 35, September: ** He was dispatched to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], where he excavated {{ill|Takaishi Pond|de|Takaishi Pond|ja|高石池|zh|Takaishi Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi Pond|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Takaishi, Osaka|Takaishi]] and {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Izumisano|Izumisano]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]] in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]]]] * Year 39, October: ** At the {{ill|Upper Uto River Shrine|de|Upper Uto River Shrine|ja|菟砥川上宮|zh|Upper Uto River Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Uto River Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Uto River basin|de|Uto River basin|ja|菟砥川流域|zh|Uto River basin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto River basin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (in [[:en:Hannan|Hannan]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]]), he forged 1,000 swords and dedicated them to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. From then on, he oversaw the shrine’s treasures{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. ** An alternate account says he had 1,000 great swords forged, receiving ten crafts offices (品部): {{ill|Tatebe|de|Tatebe|ja|楯部|zh|Tatebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Worabe|de|Worabe|ja|倭文部|zh|Worabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worabe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyugebe|de|Kamiyugebe|ja|神弓削部|zh|Kamiyugebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyugebe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyazukuri-be|de|Kamiyazukuri-be|ja|神矢作部|zh|Kamiyazukuri-be|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyazukuri-be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōanasobe|de|Ōanasobe|ja|大穴磯部|zh|Ōanasobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōanasobe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tomashikibe|de|Tomashikibe|ja|泊橿部|zh|Tomashikibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomashikibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tamakurabe|de|Tamakurabe|ja|玉作部|zh|Tamakurabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakurabe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamikōbe|de|Kamikōbe|ja|神刑部|zh|Kamikōbe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikōbe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hiokibe|de|Hiokibe|ja|日置部|zh|Hiokibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōtōhaibe|de|Ōtōhaibe|ja|大刀佩部|zh|Ōtōhaibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtōhaibe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Those swords were moved from {{ill|Oshisaka Village|de|Oshisaka Village|ja|忍坂邑|zh|Oshisaka Village|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. * Year 87, February 5: ** Due to old age, he entrusted oversight of the shrine’s treasures to his sister {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She, citing her delicate health, passed them to {{ill|Mononobe no Tokotsune|de|Mononobe no Tokotsune|ja|物部十千根|zh|Mononobe no Tokotsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tokotsune|qq=}}, establishing the Mononobe clan’s stewardship{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]].
In the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' entry for Emperor Suinin, he is credited with creating {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sayama Pond|de|Sayama Pond|ja|狭山池|zh|Sayama Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Pond|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōsakasayama|Osakasayama]], and a pond at {{ill|Kusaka|de|Kusaka|ja|日下|zh|Kusaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and with forging 1,000 swords at {{ill|Tottori River Upper Shrine|de|Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja|鳥取河上宮|zh|Tottori River Upper Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori River Upper Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]. He also organized the Kawakami tribe there{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]].
== Tomb == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Udo no Haka (宇度墓), the tomb of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto at {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]]}}]] The tomb, designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]], is the '''Udo no Haka''' (宇度墓, {{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=Udo no Haka (Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto’s tomb)}}) in {{ill|Tannowa|de|Tannowa|ja|淡輪|zh|Tannowa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa|qq=}}, [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki Town]], [[:en:Sennan District, Osaka|Sennan District]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] called {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, 200 m long.
Although not mentioned in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' or ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'' (927) lists “Udo no Haka” in [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], {{ill|Hine District|de|Hine District|ja|日根郡|zh|Hine District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine District|qq=}}, with precincts of 3 chō east–west by 3 chō north–south, guarded by 3 households, classed as a remote tomb. An earlier 691 decree established three guard households at royal tombs of merit; scholars suggest this reflects parallel tomb-designations based on older records like the ''[[:en:Teiki|Teiki]]'' and ''[[:en:Kyūji|Kyuji]]''. One theory holds that the tombs of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoisosenu no Mikoto]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Todo Chikarako|de|Todo Chikarako|ja|菟道稚郎子|zh|Todo Chikarako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todo Chikarako|qq=}} formed the four boundaries of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]<ref>{{Harvid|仁藤}}</ref>.
The tomb fell into ruin in the medieval period{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. In 1874, based on the ''{{ill|Senshūshi|de|Senshūshi|ja|泉州志|zh|Senshūshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshūshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', it was located on Mt. Tamada, but in 1880 moved to the present site{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. The ''{{ill|Izumiki|de|Izumiki|ja|和泉志|zh|Izumiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志}}'' records it as the tomb of {{ill|Ki no Koyumi|de|Ki no Koyumi|ja|紀小弓|zh|Ki no Koyumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Koyumi|qq=}}{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}.
== Legends == According to the shrine legend of [[:en:Inaba Shrine|Inaba Shrine]] in [[:en:Gifu|Gifu City]], Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto pacified Ōshū by imperial order, but Murō no Kami {{ill|Toyomasa|de|Toyomasa|ja|豊益|zh|Toyomasa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, envious of his success, slandered him as rebellious. He was then attacked as a traitor and killed there. His consort, {{ill|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|de|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|zh|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, daughter of Emperor Keikō, left the capital to mourn him here until her death.
== Notes ==
