revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|father={{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}|mother={{ill|Hibasuhimenomikoto|WD=Q8375821}}|relatives={{ill|Keikō|WD=Q329723}}; {{ill|Ojiwake no mikoto|WD=Q11620549}}; {{ill|Prince Homutsuwake|WD=Q11631423}}; {{ill|Prince Ikatarashihiko|WD=Q11372186}}; {{ill|Prince Ikohayawake|WD=Q11492324}}; {{ill|Prince Iwatsukuwake|WD=Q11587884}}; {{ill|Prince Tuburame|WD=Q11394830}}; {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732}}|native_name=五十瓊敷入彦命}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11372194]] Wikidata name: Prince Inishikiirihiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Legendary Tomb (Udo no Haka) of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto}}{{small|({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}・{{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District}}・{{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town}}の{{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}) }}]] '''Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto''' (五十瓊敷入彦命{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese Imperial family member|de|Japanese Imperial family member|ja|皇族|zh|Japanese Imperial family member|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family member|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}} known from the {{ill|Kiki|en|Kiki|WD=Q294330|lt=Kiki}} texts.
In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', he is called “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”, “Inishiki no Mikoto”, or “Inishiki Ōji”, and in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' as '''Inshiki Irihiko no Mikoto'''.
He was a son of the 11th {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} and the elder full brother of the 12th {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}. He is famous in the legends concerning the sacred treasures of {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}} in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}.
== Genealogy == {{smaller|(Names follow ''Nihon Shoki'' first, with ''Kojiki'' names in parentheses)}}
According to both the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'' and the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', he was born to {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} and the later Empress {{ill|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|de|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|zh|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|1=Hibasuhimenomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8375821}}.
Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto was the eldest, and the texts record four full siblings: * Younger brother: {{ill|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|de|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大足彦尊|zh|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the 12th '''{{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}'''. * Younger sister: {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the ''Kojiki'' names the male {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大中津日子命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Younger sister: {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamato-hime no Mikoto}}. * Younger brother: {{ill|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|de|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja|稚城瓊入彦命|zh|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Records == In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', the following deeds are recorded for Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}: * Year 30, January 6 of Emperor Suinin’s reign: ** When asked what they desired, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto chose bows and arrows, and Otari-hiko no Mikoto chose the throne. The emperor granted bows and arrows to Inishiki, and told Otari-hiko to succeed to the throne{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 35, September: ** He was dispatched to {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}, where he excavated {{ill|Takaishi Pond|de|Takaishi Pond|ja|高石池|zh|Takaishi Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|en|Takaishi, Osaka|WD=Q857218|lt=Takaishi}} and {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Izumisano|en|Izumisano|WD=Q852018|lt=Izumisano}}{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}} in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}]] * Year 39, October: ** At the {{ill|Upper Uto River Shrine|de|Upper Uto River Shrine|ja|菟砥川上宮|zh|Upper Uto River Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Uto River Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Uto River basin|de|Uto River basin|ja|菟砥川流域|zh|Uto River basin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto River basin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (in {{ill|Hannan|en|Hannan|WD=Q350174|lt=Hannan}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District}}), he forged 1,000 swords and dedicated them to {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}}. From then on, he oversaw the shrine’s treasures{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. ** An alternate account says he had 1,000 great swords forged, receiving ten crafts offices (品部): {{ill|Tatebe|de|Tatebe|ja|楯部|zh|Tatebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Worabe|de|Worabe|ja|倭文部|zh|Worabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worabe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyugebe|de|Kamiyugebe|ja|神弓削部|zh|Kamiyugebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyugebe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyazukuri-be|de|Kamiyazukuri-be|ja|神矢作部|zh|Kamiyazukuri-be|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyazukuri-be|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōanasobe|de|Ōanasobe|ja|大穴磯部|zh|Ōanasobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōanasobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tomashikibe|de|Tomashikibe|ja|泊橿部|zh|Tomashikibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomashikibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tamakurabe|de|Tamakurabe|ja|玉作部|zh|Tamakurabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakurabe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamikōbe|de|Kamikōbe|ja|神刑部|zh|Kamikōbe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikōbe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hiokibe|de|Hiokibe|ja|日置部|zh|Hiokibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōtōhaibe|de|Ōtōhaibe|ja|大刀佩部|zh|Ōtōhaibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtōhaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Those swords were moved from {{ill|Oshisaka Village|de|Oshisaka Village|ja|忍坂邑|zh|Oshisaka Village|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}}{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 87, February 5: ** Due to old age, he entrusted oversight of the shrine’s treasures to his sister {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She, citing her delicate health, passed them to {{ill|Mononobe no Tokotsune|de|Mononobe no Tokotsune|ja|物部十千根|zh|Mononobe no Tokotsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tokotsune|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24885356}}, establishing the Mononobe clan’s stewardship{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' entry for Emperor Suinin, he is credited with creating {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sayama Pond|de|Sayama Pond|ja|狭山池|zh|Sayama Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Pond|1=Sayama Pond|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571742}} in {{ill|Ōsakasayama|en|Ōsakasayama|WD=Q385864|lt=Osakasayama}}, and a pond at {{ill|Kusaka|de|Kusaka|ja|日下|zh|Kusaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and with forging 1,000 swords at {{ill|Tottori River Upper Shrine|de|Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja|鳥取河上宮|zh|Tottori River Upper Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}}. He also organized the Kawakami tribe there{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
== Tomb == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Udo no Haka (宇度墓), the tomb of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto at {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town}}}}]] The tomb, designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}, is the '''Udo no Haka''' (宇度墓, {{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=Udo no Haka (Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto’s tomb)}}) in {{ill|Tannowa|de|Tannowa|ja|淡輪|zh|Tannowa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa|1=Tannowa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559834}}, {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} called {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, 200 m long.
Although not mentioned in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'' or ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}'' (927) lists “Udo no Haka” in {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province}}, {{ill|Hine District|de|Hine District|ja|日根郡|zh|Hine District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine District|1=Hine district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509546}}, with precincts of 3 chō east–west by 3 chō north–south, guarded by 3 households, classed as a remote tomb. An earlier 691 decree established three guard households at royal tombs of merit; scholars suggest this reflects parallel tomb-designations based on older records like the ''{{ill|Teiki|en|Teiki|WD=Q1107261|lt=Teiki}}'' and ''{{ill|Kyūji|en|Kyūji|WD=Q1107210|lt=Kyuji}}''. One theory holds that the tombs of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}, {{ill|Itsuse no Mikoto|en|Itsuse no Mikoto|WD=Q10881970|lt=Hikoisosenu no Mikoto}}, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Todo Chikarako|de|Todo Chikarako|ja|菟道稚郎子|zh|Todo Chikarako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todo Chikarako|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}} formed the four boundaries of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}<ref>{{Harvid|仁藤}}</ref>.
The tomb fell into ruin in the medieval period{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. In 1874, based on the ''{{ill|Senshūshi|de|Senshūshi|ja|泉州志|zh|Senshūshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshūshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', it was located on Mt. Tamada, but in 1880 moved to the present site{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. The ''{{ill|Izumiki|de|Izumiki|ja|和泉志|zh|Izumiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372555}}'' records it as the tomb of {{ill|Ki no Koyumi|de|Ki no Koyumi|ja|紀小弓|zh|Ki no Koyumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Koyumi|1=Ki no Koyumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605024}}{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}.
== Legends == According to the shrine legend of {{ill|Inaba Shrine|en|Inaba Shrine|WD=Q701414|lt=Inaba Shrine}} in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu City}}, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto pacified Ōshū by imperial order, but Murō no Kami {{ill|Toyomasa|de|Toyomasa|ja|豊益|zh|Toyomasa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, envious of his success, slandered him as rebellious. He was then attacked as a traitor and killed there. His consort, {{ill|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|de|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|zh|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, daughter of Emperor Keikō, left the capital to mourn him here until her death.
== Notes ==

== References == * {{Cite book|和書|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|en|Kokushi Daijiten|WD=Q11420603|lt=Kokushi Daijiten (Showa era)}}|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]}} ** {{Wikicite|reference={{ill|Ueda Masaaki|de|Ueda Masaaki|ja|上田正昭|zh|Ueda Masaaki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Masaaki|1=Masaaki Ueda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3297094}} “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”|ref={{Harvid|五十瓊敷入彦命（国史）}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference={{ill|Ishii Shigesuke|de|Ishii Shigesuke|ja|石井茂輔|zh|Ishii Shigesuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shigesuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} “Udo no Haka”|ref={{Harvid|宇度墓（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|chapter=Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto (2010)|title={{ill|Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten Publishers’ Edition|de|Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten Publishers’ Edition|ja|日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|zh|Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten Publishers’ Edition|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten Publishers’ Edition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579296}}|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588}}

<!--standardizing to enwiki standards where this would merely be the intersection of two categories--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11372194}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|father={{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}|mother={{ill|Hibasuhimenomikoto|WD=Q8375821}}|relatives={{ill|Keikō|WD=Q329723}}; {{ill|Ojiwake no mikoto|WD=Q11620549}}; {{ill|Prince Homutsuwake|WD=Q11631423}}; {{ill|Prince Ikatarashihiko|WD=Q11372186}}; {{ill|Prince Ikohayawake|WD=Q11492324}}; {{ill|Prince Iwatsukuwake|WD=Q11587884}}; {{ill|Prince Tuburame|WD=Q11394830}}; {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732}}|native_name=五十瓊敷入彦命}} [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Legendary Tomb (Udo no Haka) of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto}}{{small|({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}・{{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District}}・{{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town}}の{{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}) }}]] '''Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto''' (五十瓊敷入彦命{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese Imperial family member|de|Japanese Imperial family member|ja|皇族|zh|Japanese Imperial family member|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family member|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}} known from the {{ill|Kiki|en|Kiki|WD=Q294330|lt=Kiki}} texts.
In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', he is called “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”, “Inishiki no Mikoto”, or “Inishiki Ōji”, and in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' as '''Inshiki Irihiko no Mikoto'''.
He was a son of the 11th {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} and the elder full brother of the 12th {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}. He is famous in the legends concerning the sacred treasures of {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}} in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}.
== Genealogy == {{smaller|(Names follow ''Nihon Shoki'' first, with ''Kojiki'' names in parentheses)}}
According to both the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'' and the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', he was born to {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} and the later Empress {{ill|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|de|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|zh|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|1=Hibasuhimenomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8375821}}.
Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto was the eldest, and the texts record four full siblings: * Younger brother: {{ill|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|de|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大足彦尊|zh|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the 12th '''{{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}'''. * Younger sister: {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the ''Kojiki'' names the male {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大中津日子命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Younger sister: {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamato-hime no Mikoto}}. * Younger brother: {{ill|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|de|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja|稚城瓊入彦命|zh|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Records == In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', the following deeds are recorded for Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}: * Year 30, January 6 of Emperor Suinin’s reign: ** When asked what they desired, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto chose bows and arrows, and Otari-hiko no Mikoto chose the throne. The emperor granted bows and arrows to Inishiki, and told Otari-hiko to succeed to the throne{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 35, September: ** He was dispatched to {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}, where he excavated {{ill|Takaishi Pond|de|Takaishi Pond|ja|高石池|zh|Takaishi Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|en|Takaishi, Osaka|WD=Q857218|lt=Takaishi}} and {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Izumisano|en|Izumisano|WD=Q852018|lt=Izumisano}}{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}} in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}]] * Year 39, October: ** At the {{ill|Upper Uto River Shrine|de|Upper Uto River Shrine|ja|菟砥川上宮|zh|Upper Uto River Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Uto River Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Uto River basin|de|Uto River basin|ja|菟砥川流域|zh|Uto River basin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto River basin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (in {{ill|Hannan|en|Hannan|WD=Q350174|lt=Hannan}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District}}), he forged 1,000 swords and dedicated them to {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}}. From then on, he oversaw the shrine’s treasures{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. ** An alternate account says he had 1,000 great swords forged, receiving ten crafts offices (品部): {{ill|Tatebe|de|Tatebe|ja|楯部|zh|Tatebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Worabe|de|Worabe|ja|倭文部|zh|Worabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worabe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyugebe|de|Kamiyugebe|ja|神弓削部|zh|Kamiyugebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyugebe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyazukuri-be|de|Kamiyazukuri-be|ja|神矢作部|zh|Kamiyazukuri-be|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyazukuri-be|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōanasobe|de|Ōanasobe|ja|大穴磯部|zh|Ōanasobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōanasobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tomashikibe|de|Tomashikibe|ja|泊橿部|zh|Tomashikibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomashikibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tamakurabe|de|Tamakurabe|ja|玉作部|zh|Tamakurabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakurabe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamikōbe|de|Kamikōbe|ja|神刑部|zh|Kamikōbe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikōbe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hiokibe|de|Hiokibe|ja|日置部|zh|Hiokibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōtōhaibe|de|Ōtōhaibe|ja|大刀佩部|zh|Ōtōhaibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtōhaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Those swords were moved from {{ill|Oshisaka Village|de|Oshisaka Village|ja|忍坂邑|zh|Oshisaka Village|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}}{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 87, February 5: ** Due to old age, he entrusted oversight of the shrine’s treasures to his sister {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She, citing her delicate health, passed them to {{ill|Mononobe no Tokotsune|de|Mononobe no Tokotsune|ja|物部十千根|zh|Mononobe no Tokotsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tokotsune|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24885356}}, establishing the Mononobe clan’s stewardship{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' entry for Emperor Suinin, he is credited with creating {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sayama Pond|de|Sayama Pond|ja|狭山池|zh|Sayama Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Pond|1=Sayama Pond|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571742}} in {{ill|Ōsakasayama|en|Ōsakasayama|WD=Q385864|lt=Osakasayama}}, and a pond at {{ill|Kusaka|de|Kusaka|ja|日下|zh|Kusaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and with forging 1,000 swords at {{ill|Tottori River Upper Shrine|de|Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja|鳥取河上宮|zh|Tottori River Upper Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}}. He also organized the Kawakami tribe there{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
== Tomb == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Udo no Haka (宇度墓), the tomb of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto at {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town}}}}]] The tomb, designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}, is the '''Udo no Haka''' (宇度墓, {{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=Udo no Haka (Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto’s tomb)}}) in {{ill|Tannowa|de|Tannowa|ja|淡輪|zh|Tannowa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa|1=Tannowa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559834}}, {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} called {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, 200 m long.
Although not mentioned in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'' or ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}'' (927) lists “Udo no Haka” in {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province}}, {{ill|Hine District|de|Hine District|ja|日根郡|zh|Hine District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine District|1=Hine district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509546}}, with precincts of 3 chō east–west by 3 chō north–south, guarded by 3 households, classed as a remote tomb. An earlier 691 decree established three guard households at royal tombs of merit; scholars suggest this reflects parallel tomb-designations based on older records like the ''{{ill|Teiki|en|Teiki|WD=Q1107261|lt=Teiki}}'' and ''{{ill|Kyūji|en|Kyūji|WD=Q1107210|lt=Kyuji}}''. One theory holds that the tombs of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}, {{ill|Itsuse no Mikoto|en|Itsuse no Mikoto|WD=Q10881970|lt=Hikoisosenu no Mikoto}}, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Todo Chikarako|de|Todo Chikarako|ja|菟道稚郎子|zh|Todo Chikarako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todo Chikarako|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}} formed the four boundaries of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}<ref>{{Harvid|仁藤}}</ref>.
The tomb fell into ruin in the medieval period{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. In 1874, based on the ''{{ill|Senshūshi|de|Senshūshi|ja|泉州志|zh|Senshūshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshūshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', it was located on Mt. Tamada, but in 1880 moved to the present site{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. The ''{{ill|Izumiki|de|Izumiki|ja|和泉志|zh|Izumiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372555}}'' records it as the tomb of {{ill|Ki no Koyumi|de|Ki no Koyumi|ja|紀小弓|zh|Ki no Koyumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Koyumi|1=Ki no Koyumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605024}}{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}.
== Legends == According to the shrine legend of {{ill|Inaba Shrine|en|Inaba Shrine|WD=Q701414|lt=Inaba Shrine}} in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu City}}, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto pacified Ōshū by imperial order, but Murō no Kami {{ill|Toyomasa|de|Toyomasa|ja|豊益|zh|Toyomasa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, envious of his success, slandered him as rebellious. He was then attacked as a traitor and killed there. His consort, {{ill|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|de|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|zh|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, daughter of Emperor Keikō, left the capital to mourn him here until her death.
== Notes ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|father={{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}|mother={{ill|Hibasuhimenomikoto|WD=Q8375821}}|relatives={{ill|Keikō|WD=Q329723}}; {{ill|Ojiwake no mikoto|WD=Q11620549}}; {{ill|Prince Homutsuwake|WD=Q11631423}}; {{ill|Prince Ikatarashihiko|WD=Q11372186}}; {{ill|Prince Ikohayawake|WD=Q11492324}}; {{ill|Prince Iwatsukuwake|WD=Q11587884}}; {{ill|Prince Tuburame|WD=Q11394830}}; {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732}}|native_name=五十瓊敷入彦命}} [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Legendary Tomb (Udo no Haka) of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto}}{{small|({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}・{{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District}}・{{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town}}の{{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}) }}]] '''Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto''' (五十瓊敷入彦命{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese Imperial family member|de|Japanese Imperial family member|ja|皇族|zh|Japanese Imperial family member|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family member|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}} known from the {{ill|Kiki|en|Kiki|WD=Q294330|lt=Kiki}} texts.
In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', he is called “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”, “Inishiki no Mikoto”, or “Inishiki Ōji”, and in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' as '''Inshiki Irihiko no Mikoto'''.
He was a son of the 11th {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} and the elder full brother of the 12th {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}. He is famous in the legends concerning the sacred treasures of {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}} in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}.
== Genealogy == {{smaller|(Names follow ''Nihon Shoki'' first, with ''Kojiki'' names in parentheses)}}
According to both the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'' and the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', he was born to {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} and the later Empress {{ill|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|de|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|zh|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|1=Hibasuhimenomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8375821}}.
Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto was the eldest, and the texts record four full siblings: * Younger brother: {{ill|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|de|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大足彦尊|zh|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the 12th '''{{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}'''. * Younger sister: {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the ''Kojiki'' names the male {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大中津日子命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Younger sister: {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamato-hime no Mikoto}}. * Younger brother: {{ill|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|de|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja|稚城瓊入彦命|zh|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Records == In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', the following deeds are recorded for Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}: * Year 30, January 6 of Emperor Suinin’s reign: ** When asked what they desired, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto chose bows and arrows, and Otari-hiko no Mikoto chose the throne. The emperor granted bows and arrows to Inishiki, and told Otari-hiko to succeed to the throne{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 35, September: ** He was dispatched to {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}, where he excavated {{ill|Takaishi Pond|de|Takaishi Pond|ja|高石池|zh|Takaishi Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|en|Takaishi, Osaka|WD=Q857218|lt=Takaishi}} and {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Izumisano|en|Izumisano|WD=Q852018|lt=Izumisano}}{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}} in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}]] * Year 39, October: ** At the {{ill|Upper Uto River Shrine|de|Upper Uto River Shrine|ja|菟砥川上宮|zh|Upper Uto River Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Uto River Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Uto River basin|de|Uto River basin|ja|菟砥川流域|zh|Uto River basin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto River basin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (in {{ill|Hannan|en|Hannan|WD=Q350174|lt=Hannan}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District}}), he forged 1,000 swords and dedicated them to {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}}. From then on, he oversaw the shrine’s treasures{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. ** An alternate account says he had 1,000 great swords forged, receiving ten crafts offices (品部): {{ill|Tatebe|de|Tatebe|ja|楯部|zh|Tatebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Worabe|de|Worabe|ja|倭文部|zh|Worabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worabe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyugebe|de|Kamiyugebe|ja|神弓削部|zh|Kamiyugebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyugebe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyazukuri-be|de|Kamiyazukuri-be|ja|神矢作部|zh|Kamiyazukuri-be|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyazukuri-be|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōanasobe|de|Ōanasobe|ja|大穴磯部|zh|Ōanasobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōanasobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tomashikibe|de|Tomashikibe|ja|泊橿部|zh|Tomashikibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomashikibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tamakurabe|de|Tamakurabe|ja|玉作部|zh|Tamakurabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakurabe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamikōbe|de|Kamikōbe|ja|神刑部|zh|Kamikōbe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikōbe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hiokibe|de|Hiokibe|ja|日置部|zh|Hiokibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōtōhaibe|de|Ōtōhaibe|ja|大刀佩部|zh|Ōtōhaibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtōhaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Those swords were moved from {{ill|Oshisaka Village|de|Oshisaka Village|ja|忍坂邑|zh|Oshisaka Village|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}}{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 87, February 5: ** Due to old age, he entrusted oversight of the shrine’s treasures to his sister {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She, citing her delicate health, passed them to {{ill|Mononobe no Tokotsune|de|Mononobe no Tokotsune|ja|物部十千根|zh|Mononobe no Tokotsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tokotsune|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24885356}}, establishing the Mononobe clan’s stewardship{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' entry for Emperor Suinin, he is credited with creating {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sayama Pond|de|Sayama Pond|ja|狭山池|zh|Sayama Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Pond|1=Sayama Pond|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571742}} in {{ill|Ōsakasayama|en|Ōsakasayama|WD=Q385864|lt=Osakasayama}}, and a pond at {{ill|Kusaka|de|Kusaka|ja|日下|zh|Kusaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and with forging 1,000 swords at {{ill|Tottori River Upper Shrine|de|Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja|鳥取河上宮|zh|Tottori River Upper Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}}. He also organized the Kawakami tribe there{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
== Tomb == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Udo no Haka (宇度墓), the tomb of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto at {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town}}}}]] The tomb, designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}, is the '''Udo no Haka''' (宇度墓, {{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=Udo no Haka (Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto’s tomb)}}) in {{ill|Tannowa|de|Tannowa|ja|淡輪|zh|Tannowa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa|1=Tannowa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559834}}, {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} called {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, 200 m long.
Although not mentioned in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'' or ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}'' (927) lists “Udo no Haka” in {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province}}, {{ill|Hine District|de|Hine District|ja|日根郡|zh|Hine District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine District|1=Hine district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509546}}, with precincts of 3 chō east–west by 3 chō north–south, guarded by 3 households, classed as a remote tomb. An earlier 691 decree established three guard households at royal tombs of merit; scholars suggest this reflects parallel tomb-designations based on older records like the ''{{ill|Teiki|en|Teiki|WD=Q1107261|lt=Teiki}}'' and ''{{ill|Kyūji|en|Kyūji|WD=Q1107210|lt=Kyuji}}''. One theory holds that the tombs of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}, {{ill|Itsuse no Mikoto|en|Itsuse no Mikoto|WD=Q10881970|lt=Hikoisosenu no Mikoto}}, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Todo Chikarako|de|Todo Chikarako|ja|菟道稚郎子|zh|Todo Chikarako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todo Chikarako|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}} formed the four boundaries of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}<ref>{{Harvid|仁藤}}</ref>.
The tomb fell into ruin in the medieval period{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. In 1874, based on the ''{{ill|Senshūshi|de|Senshūshi|ja|泉州志|zh|Senshūshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshūshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', it was located on Mt. Tamada, but in 1880 moved to the present site{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. The ''{{ill|Izumiki|de|Izumiki|ja|和泉志|zh|Izumiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372555}}'' records it as the tomb of {{ill|Ki no Koyumi|de|Ki no Koyumi|ja|紀小弓|zh|Ki no Koyumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Koyumi|1=Ki no Koyumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605024}}{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}.
== Legends == According to the shrine legend of {{ill|Inaba Shrine|en|Inaba Shrine|WD=Q701414|lt=Inaba Shrine}} in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu City}}, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto pacified Ōshū by imperial order, but Murō no Kami {{ill|Toyomasa|de|Toyomasa|ja|豊益|zh|Toyomasa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, envious of his success, slandered him as rebellious. He was then attacked as a traitor and killed there. His consort, {{ill|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|de|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|zh|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, daughter of Emperor Keikō, left the capital to mourn him here until her death.
== Notes ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|father={{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}|mother={{ill|Hibasuhimenomikoto|WD=Q8375821}}|relatives={{ill|Keikō|WD=Q329723}}; {{ill|Ojiwake no mikoto|WD=Q11620549}}; {{ill|Prince Homutsuwake|WD=Q11631423}}; {{ill|Prince Ikatarashihiko|WD=Q11372186}}; {{ill|Prince Ikohayawake|WD=Q11492324}}; {{ill|Prince Iwatsukuwake|WD=Q11587884}}; {{ill|Prince Tuburame|WD=Q11394830}}; {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732}}|native_name=五十瓊敷入彦命}} [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Legendary Tomb (Udo no Haka) of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto}}{{small|({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}・{{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District}}・{{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town}}の{{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}) }}]] '''Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto''' (五十瓊敷入彦命{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese Imperial family member|de|Japanese Imperial family member|ja|皇族|zh|Japanese Imperial family member|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family member|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}} known from the {{ill|Kiki|en|Kiki|WD=Q294330|lt=Kiki}} texts.
In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', he is called “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”, “Inishiki no Mikoto”, or “Inishiki Ōji”, and in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' as '''Inshiki Irihiko no Mikoto'''.
He was a son of the 11th {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} and the elder full brother of the 12th {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}. He is famous in the legends concerning the sacred treasures of {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}} in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}.
== Genealogy == {{smaller|(Names follow ''Nihon Shoki'' first, with ''Kojiki'' names in parentheses)}}
According to both the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'' and the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', he was born to {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} and the later Empress {{ill|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|de|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|zh|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|1=Hibasuhimenomikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8375821}}.
Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto was the eldest, and the texts record four full siblings: * Younger brother: {{ill|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|de|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大足彦尊|zh|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the 12th '''{{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}'''. * Younger sister: {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the ''Kojiki'' names the male {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大中津日子命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Younger sister: {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamato-hime no Mikoto}}. * Younger brother: {{ill|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|de|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja|稚城瓊入彦命|zh|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Records == In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', the following deeds are recorded for Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}: * Year 30, January 6 of Emperor Suinin’s reign: ** When asked what they desired, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto chose bows and arrows, and Otari-hiko no Mikoto chose the throne. The emperor granted bows and arrows to Inishiki, and told Otari-hiko to succeed to the throne{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 35, September: ** He was dispatched to {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}, where he excavated {{ill|Takaishi Pond|de|Takaishi Pond|ja|高石池|zh|Takaishi Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|en|Takaishi, Osaka|WD=Q857218|lt=Takaishi}} and {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Izumisano|en|Izumisano|WD=Q852018|lt=Izumisano}}{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}} in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri}}]] * Year 39, October: ** At the {{ill|Upper Uto River Shrine|de|Upper Uto River Shrine|ja|菟砥川上宮|zh|Upper Uto River Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Uto River Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Uto River basin|de|Uto River basin|ja|菟砥川流域|zh|Uto River basin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto River basin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (in {{ill|Hannan|en|Hannan|WD=Q350174|lt=Hannan}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District}}), he forged 1,000 swords and dedicated them to {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}}. From then on, he oversaw the shrine’s treasures{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. ** An alternate account says he had 1,000 great swords forged, receiving ten crafts offices (品部): {{ill|Tatebe|de|Tatebe|ja|楯部|zh|Tatebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Worabe|de|Worabe|ja|倭文部|zh|Worabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worabe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyugebe|de|Kamiyugebe|ja|神弓削部|zh|Kamiyugebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyugebe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyazukuri-be|de|Kamiyazukuri-be|ja|神矢作部|zh|Kamiyazukuri-be|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyazukuri-be|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōanasobe|de|Ōanasobe|ja|大穴磯部|zh|Ōanasobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōanasobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tomashikibe|de|Tomashikibe|ja|泊橿部|zh|Tomashikibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomashikibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tamakurabe|de|Tamakurabe|ja|玉作部|zh|Tamakurabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakurabe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamikōbe|de|Kamikōbe|ja|神刑部|zh|Kamikōbe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikōbe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hiokibe|de|Hiokibe|ja|日置部|zh|Hiokibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōtōhaibe|de|Ōtōhaibe|ja|大刀佩部|zh|Ōtōhaibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtōhaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Those swords were moved from {{ill|Oshisaka Village|de|Oshisaka Village|ja|忍坂邑|zh|Oshisaka Village|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}}{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 87, February 5: ** Due to old age, he entrusted oversight of the shrine’s treasures to his sister {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She, citing her delicate health, passed them to {{ill|Mononobe no Tokotsune|de|Mononobe no Tokotsune|ja|物部十千根|zh|Mononobe no Tokotsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tokotsune|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24885356}}, establishing the Mononobe clan’s stewardship{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' entry for Emperor Suinin, he is credited with creating {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sayama Pond|de|Sayama Pond|ja|狭山池|zh|Sayama Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Pond|1=Sayama Pond|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571742}} in {{ill|Ōsakasayama|en|Ōsakasayama|WD=Q385864|lt=Osakasayama}}, and a pond at {{ill|Kusaka|de|Kusaka|ja|日下|zh|Kusaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and with forging 1,000 swords at {{ill|Tottori River Upper Shrine|de|Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja|鳥取河上宮|zh|Tottori River Upper Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine}}. He also organized the Kawakami tribe there{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
== Tomb == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Udo no Haka (宇度墓), the tomb of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto at {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town}}}}]] The tomb, designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}}, is the '''Udo no Haka''' (宇度墓, {{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=Udo no Haka (Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto’s tomb)}}) in {{ill|Tannowa|de|Tannowa|ja|淡輪|zh|Tannowa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa|1=Tannowa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559834}}, {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} called {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, 200 m long.
Although not mentioned in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'' or ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'', the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}'' (927) lists “Udo no Haka” in {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province}}, {{ill|Hine District|de|Hine District|ja|日根郡|zh|Hine District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine District|1=Hine district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509546}}, with precincts of 3 chō east–west by 3 chō north–south, guarded by 3 households, classed as a remote tomb. An earlier 691 decree established three guard households at royal tombs of merit; scholars suggest this reflects parallel tomb-designations based on older records like the ''{{ill|Teiki|en|Teiki|WD=Q1107261|lt=Teiki}}'' and ''{{ill|Kyūji|en|Kyūji|WD=Q1107210|lt=Kyuji}}''. One theory holds that the tombs of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}, {{ill|Itsuse no Mikoto|en|Itsuse no Mikoto|WD=Q10881970|lt=Hikoisosenu no Mikoto}}, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Todo Chikarako|de|Todo Chikarako|ja|菟道稚郎子|zh|Todo Chikarako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todo Chikarako|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}} formed the four boundaries of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}<ref>{{Harvid|仁藤}}</ref>.
The tomb fell into ruin in the medieval period{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. In 1874, based on the ''{{ill|Senshūshi|de|Senshūshi|ja|泉州志|zh|Senshūshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshūshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', it was located on Mt. Tamada, but in 1880 moved to the present site{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. The ''{{ill|Izumiki|de|Izumiki|ja|和泉志|zh|Izumiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372555}}'' records it as the tomb of {{ill|Ki no Koyumi|de|Ki no Koyumi|ja|紀小弓|zh|Ki no Koyumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Koyumi|1=Ki no Koyumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605024}}{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}.
== Legends == According to the shrine legend of {{ill|Inaba Shrine|en|Inaba Shrine|WD=Q701414|lt=Inaba Shrine}} in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu City}}, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto pacified Ōshū by imperial order, but Murō no Kami {{ill|Toyomasa|de|Toyomasa|ja|豊益|zh|Toyomasa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, envious of his success, slandered him as rebellious. He was then attacked as a traitor and killed there. His consort, {{ill|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|de|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|zh|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, daughter of Emperor Keikō, left the capital to mourn him here until her death.
== Notes ==
{{reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|en|Kokushi Daijiten|WD=Q11420603|lt=Kokushi Daijiten (Showa era)}}|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]}} ** {{Wikicite|reference={{ill|Ueda Masaaki|de|Ueda Masaaki|ja|上田正昭|zh|Ueda Masaaki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Masaaki|1=Masaaki Ueda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3297094}} “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”|ref={{Harvid|五十瓊敷入彦命（国史）}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference={{ill|Ishii Shigesuke|de|Ishii Shigesuke|ja|石井茂輔|zh|Ishii Shigesuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shigesuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} “Udo no Haka”|ref={{Harvid|宇度墓（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|chapter=Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto (2010)|title={{ill|Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten Publishers’ Edition|de|Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten Publishers’ Edition|ja|日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|zh|Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten Publishers’ Edition|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten Publishers’ Edition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579296}}|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五十瓊敷入彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|father={{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}|mother={{ill|Hibasuhimenomikoto|WD=Q8375821|1=Hibasu-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Keikō|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}; {{ill|Ojiwake no mikoto|WD=Q11620549|1=Ojiwake no mikoto}}; {{ill|Prince Homutsuwake|WD=Q11631423|1=Homutsuwake no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Prince Ikatarashihiko|WD=Q11372186|1=Prince Ikatarashihiko}}; {{ill|Prince Ikohayawake|WD=Q11492324|1=Prince Ikobayawake no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Prince Iwatsukuwake|WD=Q11587884|1=Iwatsukiwake no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Prince Tuburame|WD=Q11394830|1=Prince Tuburame}}; {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}|native_name=五十瓊敷入彦命}} [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Legendary Tomb (Udo no Haka) of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto}}{{small|({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}・{{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District|1=Sennan District, Osaka}}・{{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town|1=Misaki, Osaka}}の{{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}) }}]] '''Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto''' (五十瓊敷入彦命{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese Imperial family member|de|Japanese Imperial family member|ja|皇族|zh|Japanese Imperial family member|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family member|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}} known from the {{ill|Kiki|en|Kiki|WD=Q294330|lt=Kiki|1=Kiki}} texts.
In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', he is called “Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto”, “Inishiki no Mikoto”, or “Inishiki Ōji”, and in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'' as '''Inshiki Irihiko no Mikoto'''.
He was a son of the 11th {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} and the elder full brother of the 12th {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}. He is famous in the legends concerning the sacred treasures of {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}} in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}.
== Genealogy == {{smaller|(Names follow ''Nihon Shoki'' first, with ''Kojiki'' names in parentheses)}}
According to both the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' and the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', he was born to {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} and the later Empress {{ill|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|de|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|zh|Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibasu-hime no Mikoto|1=Hibasu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8375821}}.
Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto was the eldest, and the texts record four full siblings: * Younger brother: {{ill|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|de|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大足彦尊|zh|Otari-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otari-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – the 12th '''{{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}'''. * Younger sister: {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (the ''Kojiki'' names the male {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja|大中津日子命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). * Younger sister: {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamato-hime no Mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. * Younger brother: {{ill|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|de|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja|稚城瓊入彦命|zh|Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakaki Niiri-hiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Records == In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', the following deeds are recorded for Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}: * Year 30, January 6 of Emperor Suinin’s reign: ** When asked what they desired, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto chose bows and arrows, and Otari-hiko no Mikoto chose the throne. The emperor granted bows and arrows to Inishiki, and told Otari-hiko to succeed to the throne{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 35, September: ** He was dispatched to {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, where he excavated {{ill|Takaishi Pond|de|Takaishi Pond|ja|高石池|zh|Takaishi Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaishi Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Takaishi, Osaka|en|Takaishi, Osaka|WD=Q857218|lt=Takaishi|1=Takaishi, Osaka}} and {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Izumisano|en|Izumisano|WD=Q852018|lt=Izumisano|1=Izumisano}}{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. [[File:Isonokami-jingu, haiden-2.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}} in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}]] * Year 39, October: ** At the {{ill|Upper Uto River Shrine|de|Upper Uto River Shrine|ja|菟砥川上宮|zh|Upper Uto River Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Uto River Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Uto River basin|de|Uto River basin|ja|菟砥川流域|zh|Uto River basin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uto River basin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (in {{ill|Hannan|en|Hannan|WD=Q350174|lt=Hannan|1=Hannan}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District|1=Sennan District, Osaka}}), he forged 1,000 swords and dedicated them to {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}}. From then on, he oversaw the shrine’s treasures{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. ** An alternate account says he had 1,000 great swords forged, receiving ten crafts offices (品部): {{ill|Tatebe|de|Tatebe|ja|楯部|zh|Tatebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatebe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Worabe|de|Worabe|ja|倭文部|zh|Worabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worabe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyugebe|de|Kamiyugebe|ja|神弓削部|zh|Kamiyugebe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyugebe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamiyazukuri-be|de|Kamiyazukuri-be|ja|神矢作部|zh|Kamiyazukuri-be|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyazukuri-be|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōanasobe|de|Ōanasobe|ja|大穴磯部|zh|Ōanasobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōanasobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tomashikibe|de|Tomashikibe|ja|泊橿部|zh|Tomashikibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomashikibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tamakurabe|de|Tamakurabe|ja|玉作部|zh|Tamakurabe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakurabe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamikōbe|de|Kamikōbe|ja|神刑部|zh|Kamikōbe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikōbe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hiokibe|de|Hiokibe|ja|日置部|zh|Hiokibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ōtōhaibe|de|Ōtōhaibe|ja|大刀佩部|zh|Ōtōhaibe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtōhaibe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Those swords were moved from {{ill|Oshisaka Village|de|Oshisaka Village|ja|忍坂邑|zh|Oshisaka Village|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshisaka Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}}{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}. * Year 87, February 5: ** Due to old age, he entrusted oversight of the shrine’s treasures to his sister {{ill|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|de|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja|大中姫命|zh|Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatsu-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. She, citing her delicate health, passed them to {{ill|Mononobe no Tokotsune|de|Mononobe no Tokotsune|ja|物部十千根|zh|Mononobe no Tokotsune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tokotsune|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24885356}}, establishing the Mononobe clan’s stewardship{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'' entry for Emperor Suinin, he is credited with creating {{ill|Chinui Pond|de|Chinui Pond|ja|茅渟池|zh|Chinui Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinui Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sayama Pond|de|Sayama Pond|ja|狭山池|zh|Sayama Pond|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayama Pond|1=Q11571742|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571742}} in {{ill|Ōsakasayama|en|Ōsakasayama|WD=Q385864|lt=Osakasayama|1=Ōsakasayama}}, and a pond at {{ill|Kusaka|de|Kusaka|ja|日下|zh|Kusaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and with forging 1,000 swords at {{ill|Tottori River Upper Shrine|de|Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja|鳥取河上宮|zh|Tottori River Upper Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottori River Upper Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}}. He also organized the Kawakami tribe there{{Sfn|五十瓊敷入彦命（古代氏族）|2010年}}.
== Tomb == [[File:Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun haisho-1.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Udo no Haka (宇度墓), the tomb of Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto at {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town|1=Misaki, Osaka}}}}]] The tomb, designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}, is the '''Udo no Haka''' (宇度墓, {{Coord|34|19|49.67|N|135|10|43.49|E|region:JP-27|name=Udo no Haka (Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto’s tomb)}}) in {{ill|Tannowa|de|Tannowa|ja|淡輪|zh|Tannowa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa|1=Tannowa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559834}}, {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki Town|1=Misaki, Osaka}}, {{ill|Sennan District, Osaka|en|Sennan District, Osaka|WD=Q1205635|lt=Sennan District|1=Sennan District, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} called {{ill|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|de|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|ja|淡輪ニサンザイ古墳|zh|Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun|qq=}}, 200 m long.
Although not mentioned in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' or ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}'' (927) lists “Udo no Haka” in {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}}, {{ill|Hine District|de|Hine District|ja|日根郡|zh|Hine District|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hine District|1=Hine district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509546}}, with precincts of 3 chō east–west by 3 chō north–south, guarded by 3 households, classed as a remote tomb. An earlier 691 decree established three guard households at royal tombs of merit; scholars suggest this reflects parallel tomb-designations based on older records like the ''{{ill|Teiki|en|Teiki|WD=Q1107261|lt=Teiki|1=Teiki}}'' and ''{{ill|Kyūji|en|Kyūji|WD=Q1107210|lt=Kyuji|1=Kyūji}}''. One theory holds that the tombs of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}, {{ill|Itsuse no Mikoto|en|Itsuse no Mikoto|WD=Q10881970|lt=Hikoisosenu no Mikoto|1=Itsuse no Mikoto}}, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto, and {{ill|Todo Chikarako|de|Todo Chikarako|ja|菟道稚郎子|zh|Todo Chikarako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todo Chikarako|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}} formed the four boundaries of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}<ref>{{Harvid|仁藤}}</ref>.
The tomb fell into ruin in the medieval period{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. In 1874, based on the ''{{ill|Senshūshi|de|Senshūshi|ja|泉州志|zh|Senshūshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senshūshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', it was located on Mt. Tamada, but in 1880 moved to the present site{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}. The ''{{ill|Izumiki|de|Izumiki|ja|和泉志|zh|Izumiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五畿内志|1=Five Kinai Chronicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372555}}'' records it as the tomb of {{ill|Ki no Koyumi|de|Ki no Koyumi|ja|紀小弓|zh|Ki no Koyumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Koyumi|1=Ki no Koyumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605024}}{{Sfn|宇度墓（国史）}}.
== Legends == According to the shrine legend of {{ill|Inaba Shrine|en|Inaba Shrine|WD=Q701414|lt=Inaba Shrine|1=Inaba Shrine}} in {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|WD=Q45798|lt=Gifu City|1=Gifu}}, Inishiki Irihiko no Mikoto pacified Ōshū by imperial order, but Murō no Kami {{ill|Toyomasa|de|Toyomasa|ja|豊益|zh|Toyomasa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyomasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, envious of his success, slandered him as rebellious. He was then attacked as a traitor and killed there. His consort, {{ill|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|de|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|ja|渟熨斗姫命|zh|Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunoshi-hime no Mikoto|1=Nunoshihime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272539}}, daughter of Emperor Keikō, left the capital to mourn him here until her death.
